value: Elder Dragons defy typical classification and reside outside of the standard ecosystem. These rare creatures possess immense power and have lived since ancient times. Unlike other monster types, which categorize by shared traits, Elder Dragons stand unique. They often resemble dragons but exhibit traits that do not fit any known category. Their immense power and longevity set them apart from all other monsters.
title: Elder Dragons

value: Flying Wyverns are large, bipedal monsters with two wings. Known for their mastery of flight, most have powerful wings for sustained flight. However, some are quadrupedal, using wings as forearms for enhanced land movement. Others are flightless or wingless despite their classification. These variations make Flying Wyverns adaptable and diverse, dominating the monster population in the world.
title: Flying Wyverns

value: Bird Wyverns are small to medium-sized, bipedal creatures with slender bodies. They are divided into two types: Flying Bird Wyverns and Theropod Bird Wyverns. Flying Bird Wyverns have developed wings and sharp beaks, behaving like birds. Theropod Bird Wyverns are flightless, with long tails and powerful legs, living in a social hierarchy led by a dominant alpha male. Both types exhibit unique characteristics, making them adaptable and diverse within their ecosystem.
title: Bird Wyverns

value: Leviathans are large wyverns with lithe, serpentine bodies and exceptional speed. They dominate aquatic environments like swamps and marshes, but some adapt to land by 'swimming' through sand, snow, or lava. As top predators, they outclass many other monsters in their ecosystems.
title: Leviathans

value: Brute Wyverns are flightless monsters resembling non-fictional theropods. They use powerful hindlimbs to move around and have small forelimbs. Most Brute Wyverns can barely use their forelimbs, but some have developed them for combat. These monsters are well adapted for life on land and rely on their robust legs for movement.
title: Brute Wyverns

value: Fanged Beasts are beastly, mammalian creatures that lack wings, though some have the ability to glide. They are often much faster than other larger threats. Known for their sharp fangs and agile movements, they dominate both land and aerial environments with their unique gliding capabilities. Fanged Beasts adapt quickly to their surroundings, making them formidable predators in various ecosystems.
title: Fanged Beasts

value: Fanged Wyverns are quadrupedal monsters with wingless forelimbs larger than their hindlimbs. Some, like Odogaron and Tobi-Kadachi, stand on their limbs, while others, like Great Jagras and Dodogama, drag their bodies with limbs spread to the sides. Despite their differences, many Fanged Wyverns exhibit agility and speed, though some are less nimble.
title: Fanged Wyverns

value: Hunters in HR 1-3 face beginner-level quests. At HR 1, Hunters gain access to introductory quests with easier monsters. As they progress to HR 2, quests offer moderate challenges. By HR 3, Hunters encounter tougher monsters and more complex objectives. The items available to Hunters increase in quality, allowing them to take on more dangerous foes. Hunters can join and initiate online quests within their rank, expanding their experience and earning higher rewards. Achieving these ranks prepares Hunters for more demanding adventures.
title: HR 1-3

value: Hunters reaching HR 4-7 unlock challenging quests and rare items. They gain access to higher-level armor and weapons in shops. New monsters and locations become available, including the Frostpeak Highlands and the Volcanic Region. Hunters can now join more difficult online quests and face formidable foes like the Rathalos and Diablos. These ranks require skill and strategy, pushing hunters to master their combat techniques and teamwork. Completing quests in this range often rewards unique materials and gear, essential for progressing further.
title: HR 4-7

value: Hunters in HR 7 and above face the most challenging quests. These quests feature formidable monsters with complex attack patterns and high damage output. Hunters gain access to elite items and rare materials, allowing them to craft top-tier weapons and armor. Joining online quests at this rank requires significant skill and experience. Achieving HR 7 and above signifies a Hunter's mastery and readiness for the most dangerous adventures.
title: HR 7 and above

value: The Hunter's Guild is a central governing association dedicated to maintaining ecological balance and preventing species extinction. It comprises skilled hunters and researchers who conduct quests to protect the environment from monster threats. The Guild enforces strict rules to ensure fair hunting practices and sustainable resource use. It offers training, resources, and a ranking system for hunters to grow in skill. The Guild also collaborates with local communities, fostering mutual understanding and cooperation.
title: The Hunter's Guild

value: Wyverians possess long pointed ears and four digits on their hands and feet. Their lifespan is extremely long, potentially immortal. Originating from wyverns, they exhibit human-like features, with some retaining scales and claw-like nails on their extremities. They excel in advanced technologies, dominating complex blacksmithing. Wyverians hold top social positions, often leading in the Guild and as merchants. Their appearance varies greatly, with young ones roughly human-sized, adults taller, and elders short.
title: Wyverians

value: Troverians are a short and stocky race of extremely industrious blacksmiths, miners, and builders. Although quite adept in their own right, they revere Wyverian blacksmiths for their skill, and claim to have learned blacksmithing from Wyverians in the distant past. Little else is known about them, as they tend to be rare outside of their native home in Harth and do not often venture afar.
title: Troverians

value: Felynes are small cat-like people, typically beige with brown markings, though colors and patterns vary. They live in small villages with their close cousins, the Melynx. Felynes pass down tales orally, paint pictograms, and build crude statues from rocks. Extremely hardy, they burrow into cliffs and construct buildings using natural materials. Integrated into human and Wyverian society, they often serve as cooks, cleaners, salespeople, or farmhands. Some become Palicos, aiding hunters. They are considered the lowest caste but are valued for their resourcefulness and companionship.
title: Felynes

value: Melynx are the close cousins of Felynes, known for their black and white fur with black and pink markings. They wear handkerchiefs and pouches made of woven green grasses and carry cat paw staves. Melynx are notorious thieves, often stealing from hunters in the field without provocation. Despite their kleptomania, they do not steal from members of their own village or hunters visiting their villages. Melynx share a love for felvine, a plant with strong catnip-like properties, which hunters can use to their advantage.
title: Melynx

value: Shakalakas are small, green-skinned, goblin-like creatures, distantly related to Felynes and Melynx. Known for wearing masks and their dancing, they are extremely aggressive and attack hunters who wander too close. Living in primitive tribal societies, each tribe is headed by a larger King Shakalaka who wears a flaming barbecue spit as a crown. Shakalakas change masks quickly to hide their faces, and young ones quest for their lifelong mask upon reaching adulthood.
title: Shakalakas

value: Kirin resembles a unicorn with a lion-like face and scales instead of fur. It has a mane of fur, a bushy tail tip, and tufts of fur on its shoulders and hooves. Black stripes run along its sides and legs, and its eyes are crimson. Kirin crackles with stray electricity and can summon lightning. It wreaths itself in lightning to harden its hide. Kirin relies on lightning strikes and its agility to attack, rarely using physical attacks. It can run circles around foes, gore them with its horn, and quickly dart away.
title: Kirin

value: Lao-Shan Lung bears a hulking copper red body with a cream-colored underbelly. It has numerous dark grey spikes on its neck, back, and tail. Its limbs are comparatively small with dark grey hands and feet. Its head features eight large horns that curve backwards and a smaller horn on the front that points upwards and curves back slightly. Its mouth has small teeth with several larger teeth on the outside. Lao-Shan Lung relies solely on its immense physical bulk and strength, being strong enough to smash through huge forts and barrel straight through villages and towns without a second thought.
title: Lao-Shan Lung

value: Ashen Lao-Shan Lung has a muted grey-blue carapace from years of volcanic debris. It retains the immense strength of regular Lao-Shan Lung, capable of crushing cities. Its body is harder to penetrate due to solidified ash and soot. Extremely docile, it only attacks when feeding is disrupted. It ignores hunters but destroys structures in its path.
title: Ashen Lao-Shan Lung

value: Fatalis resembles a traditional European dragon, with a long, serpentine neck and tail, along with a pair of large, yet muscular wings. Its body is covered in rigid black scales and lined with white spines along its back. Fatalis can destroy kingdoms with its flame breath, which can produce streams of fire or explosive blasts. It can radiate heat to melt metal and pin down foes. Fatalis exhibits extreme strength and stamina, using its whip-like tail, powerful jaws, and limbs to batter attackers. It also possesses extraordinary self-recovery abilities.
title: Fatalis

value: Crimson Fatalis is a Fatalis whose scales have turned a reddish-crimson from generations in the Volcano. Its body is mostly black, but red scales cover its chest, neck, undertail, and wing tips. One horn is extremely overgrown. Known for its extreme aggression, Crimson Fatalis attacks without hesitation, driven by eternal rage. It inhabits the Volcanic Belt, blending into the fiery environment. Its natural element is lava, and it uses this to incinerate anything in its path.
title: Crimson Fatalis

value: White Fatalis stands out with luminous white scales and a line of fur down its back and tail. Its antler-like horns and less reptilian head set it apart from its relatives. When enraged, its throat and chest glow red with red lightning sparks. It can summon red lightning storms, produce electrical projectiles, and imbue its claws with red lightning. Observed in remote areas like Tower and Castle Schrade, its natural environment remains unknown.
title: White Fatalis

value: Kushala Daora is a dragon with metallic skin, large wings, and backward-curving horns. It manipulates wind to create tornadoes and violent storms, and generates a wind barrier that strengthens with anger. This barrier can deflect attacks and blow back hunters. The dragon can inflict ailments like frostbite with freezing winds. Poison or a broken horn can weaken its wind barrier. Kushala Daora can also produce Dragon element to empower its physical attacks.
title: Kushala Daora

value: Rusted Kushala Daora is an Elder Dragon with rusted metallic skin. Unlike its normal counterpart, it behaves aggressively, using physical attacks like biting and charging. It stays on the ground more often and uses its aerial turnaround bite frequently. After jumping back, it can immediately attack with a charge or wind blast, skipping the usual taunt. Its roar and screeches are distinct from those of Kushala Daora.
title: Rusted Kushala Daora

value: Chameleos has a purple and white hide and a horn at the tip of its nose. Its large, orb-like eyes can move independently, and its long tongue can steal items from a distance. It uses its purple wings to control poison fog and its leaf-like tail to fan high-pressure winds. Chameleos camouflages itself using electric currents, but loses this ability if its horn breaks or tail severs. It creates thick fog, toxic clouds, acid blobs, and fatigue-inducing gas. Unpredictable in battle, it steals items and poisons hunters, though it prefers to avoid combat unless necessary.
title: Chameleos

value: Lunastra is a dragon with leonine features and a cool coloration. She has a prominent mane, large fangs, and a distinct crest that resembles a crown. Her wings are covered in fur. Lunastra uses her Flame Sac to produce a fire breathing effect. She can create highly flammable powder and spread it using her fur-covered wings. She then ignites the powder by gnashing her teeth to create sparks, causing explosions around her. The range of the explosions varies based on the color of the sparks, with red sparks indicating close-range explosions and yellow sparks indicating distant ones.
title: Lunastra

value: Amatsu, an Elder Dragon, boasts a pale white body with golden horns and flesh tendrils. It has vestigial feet and nearly useless back legs but uses its finned limbs to swim through the sky. Amatsu controls storms, summoning gales, tornadoes, and torrential rains. It fires pressurized water blasts and harnesses lightning. Its abilities intensify with injury. Amatsu's presence causes catastrophic damage, driving other monsters away and threatening entire cities.
title: Amatsu

value: Teostra is a large dragon with leonine features and a fiery red coloration. It has a grand mane, large fangs, and curved horns. Its fur-covered wings and tail make it distinct. Teostra breathes powerful streams of fire and creates explosive powder from its scales. It attacks intruders ruthlessly and cooperates with Lunastra. Older Teostra may be calmer but still fiercely defend their territory.
title: Teostra

value: Yama Tsukami is an Elder Dragon with an octopus-like body, covered in moss, algae, and other plant life. It has four thick tentacles, two large whiskers, and human-like teeth and gums. Yama Tsukami produces gas to keep itself afloat and float midair. It has a symbiotic relationship with Great Thunderbugs, which it shelters and which explode to attack threats. Yama Tsukami releases burning gas from its pores and can travel long distances without stopping. It becomes docile after feeding but becomes hostile if cornered.
title: Yama Tsukami

value: Alatreon is a regal, quadrupedal Elder Dragon covered in backward-facing black scales with a blade-like texture. It has spiked armor on its chest, powerful limbs with sharp claws, and a long, muscular tail. Its head features a beaklike mouth and two large, serrated horns that curve outward. Alatreon controls all elements: Dragon, Fire, Ice, Thunder, and Water. It shoots flame, freezes air, summons lightning, sprays flammable fluid, and infuses its body with draconic energy. Breaking its horns temporarily weakens its elemental powers.
title: Alatreon

value: Jhen Mohran is an extremely large monster, with a long, almost serpentine body shape. It has sturdy, sharp-ended tusks on its head and a rocky spine with valuable minerals along its back. Due to vestigial back legs, it moves by dragging its body or swimming in the sand. Normally calm, Jhen Mohran attacks approaching Dragonships by ramming them with its body and throwing stones from its back. It largely ignores hunters climbing on its back, focusing instead on the Dragonship. Jhen Mohran only inhabits the Great Desert.
title: Jhen Mohran

value: The Hallowed Jhen Mohran stands slightly taller than its common counterpart, distinguished by its deep violet ore-like hide. Its strength surpasses the average Jhen Mohran, capable of withstanding and inflicting higher damage. It remains calm unless provoked, but fiercely retaliates against Desert/Dragon Ships it perceives as rivals. Found in the Great Desert, it exhibits unique patterns of attack and movement, distinct from its kin.
title: Hallowed Jhen Mohran

value: Ceadeus resembles a giant whale with a face similar to a Dunkleosteus. It has a prominent beard of fur and two massive horns, which constantly grow and eventually overtake the creature’s eyes, leaving it blind. Luminous organs line the monster's underside as well as the top of its mouth, which turn from blue to red when it is enraged. Ceadeus can cause rubble to fall, smash open large walls, and even cause earthquakes by ramming its horns against the walls of the caverns it inhabits. It can also expel large quantities of water from its mouth as a powerful beam.
title: Ceadeus

value: The Goldbeard Ceadeus, distinguishable by its darker coloring, boasts a lustrous golden hide and darker brown underbelly. Unlike the regular Ceadeus, both of its large horns are symmetrical, overtaking its single remaining eye. Notably, these horns can break off, yielding a total of four carves. Found in the deep ocean, this subspecies displays greater strength and more aggressive behavior.
title: Goldbeard Ceadeus

value: Dire Miralis is a very large, semi-aquatic Elder Dragon. Its body structure is reminiscent of Fatalis, with relatively short limbs and a long tail and neck. Its body is filled with a magma-like substance which pools at the base of the tail, the chest, and at each shoulder. Each of these pools directs magma flow to certain parts of the body. On its back are a pair of wing-like appendages. Seemingly useless in terms of flight, these 'wings' feature cannon-like chutes which can extrude globules of magma, both as a means of defense and as a way to jettison excess magma. Dire Miralis's head features a frill-like structure and an upturned snout.
title: Dire Miralis

value: Oroshi Kirin has a dark navy blue body with violet stripes, a black mane with light purple tips, and luminous red eyes. Its horn is larger with a pinkish color and a spiraling ridge. Oroshi Kirin is more aggressive than the standard Kirin and summons ice shards for defense. It can only be found in the Everwood or Tower Summit.
title: Oroshi Kirin

value: Shagaru Magala is the mature form of Gore Magala, with a body covered in glittering golden scales and dark brown horns, claws, and spiky protrusions. Its wings now resemble golden scaly sheets shaped like stars when expanded. Shagaru Magala is stronger, more agile, and ferocious, using enhanced physical attacks and Frenzy Virus abilities like explosive scale pools and ground energy eruptions. It can also enter a True Frenzy state, where its pale areas turn purple, and Frenzy Crystals form on its neck and horns, accompanied by a light purple and white aura.
title: Shagaru Magala

value: Dah'ren Mohran is a large, whale-like dragon covered in jagged armor plating. Its forelegs and back feature spine-like protrusions, and its face boasts a single gigantic, knife-like horn extending several meters. Unlike its relative Jhen Mohran, Dah'ren Mohran's body is more aggressive in appearance. It uses its horn to spear prey and its spines to defend against predators. Dah'ren Mohran's primary goal is to protect its territory, and it becomes extremely aggressive when threatened. It is known to create powerful shockwaves by slamming its body onto the ground.
title: Dah'ren Mohran

value: Dalamadur is a massive serpentine Elder Dragon with a snake-like face, bright red eyes, numerous fangs, and hook-like spikes along its body. It has clawed forearms, small hind spurs, and glowing chest vents. Dalamadur tunnels through stone with ease and can cause massive tremors. It immobilizes foes with its paralytic tongue and breathes a burning substance. It also summons meteor-like projectiles. Living in the Heavenly Mountains, Dalamadur violently defends its territory, causing falling debris with its burrowing.
title: Dalamadur

value: Shah Dalamadur appears as a Dalamadur with a white tone, red-orange spines, and enlarged facial spikes. It is the same size as a regular Dalamadur. This monster has shed its skin due to age and shares abilities like immense size, paralytic tongue, burning breath, and summoning meteors. However, its recent molting leaves it unable to control body temperature, fluctuating wildly between hardened resistance when cold and vulnerability with extreme heat when hot. Shah Dalamadur exhibits increased aggression likely due to feeling vulnerable after shedding its skin.
title: Shah Dalamadur

value: Gogmazios is an Elder Dragon with a massive body, resembling Gore Magala and Shagaru Magala, but with larger wingarms. It has red eyes, rows of sharp teeth, and a glowing pattern on its chest that looks like a scowl. Its body, covered in indigo scales and spines, secretes sticky tar to immobilize prey. An ancient dragonator is lodged in its back. Despite its size, it can fly due to powerful wings and a light skeleton. Gogmazios fires superheated oil beams and uses its size to batter opponents. It eats sulfur and gunpowder, raiding armories aggressively.
title: Gogmazios

value: Nakarkos is a massive cephalopod-like monster with glowing yellow eyes and a sharp beak. Its blue skin features turquoise bioluminescent spots. Nakarkos covers itself in self-made bones for protection and camouflage. Its two primary tentacles, tipped with skulls, resemble a two-headed skeletal dragon. Nakarkos can coat enemies in sticky mucus, burrow through earth, and swim adeptly. It replaces its tentacle skulls with body parts from other monsters, gaining new abilities. Nakarkos fires Dragon energy beams and devours entire ecosystems, including powerful Wyverns, to feed its insatiable appetite.
title: Nakarkos

value: Valstrax, a slender Elder Dragon, is covered in silver scales. It uses its jet-engine-like chest vents to generate thrust. Valstrax has fused trident wings that can expel Dragon element flames, manipulate air flow for flight and attacks, and even create massive explosions. Known for its incredible agility and high speed, it executes its deadliest move, 'Around the World,' by flying as a blazing red comet and hitting the ground at breakneck velocity. Valstrax is a highly maneuverable, durable adversary with versatile attacks, making it a formidable foe in any engagement.
title: Valstrax

value: Narwa the Allmother is an apex serpent with dark purple skin and bright purple elongated tails and thundersacs. Her eyes alternate between yellow and red. She achieves this form by consuming Wind Serpent Ibushi's lifeforce, granting her control over wind and enhanced electricity. Narwa summons stronger, faster electrical attacks and powerful wind blasts. She can lift Dragonators magnetically and summon floating stone platforms. She fertilizes her eggs in this state and attacks similarly to her original form but with greater power and range.
title: Narwa the Allmother

value: Thunder Serpent Narwa is an Elder Dragon with a leviathan-like body and short, fin-like hind legs. It features double jaws and multiple sacs along its body. Narwa has golden and orange tendrils running from head to tail and large, backward-facing horns. It uses its thunder sacs and electromagnetic force to levitate and fly. Narwa prefers long-range attacks using electricity, such as blasts and beams, and can magnetically manipulate metal objects. Its body builds up electricity in battle, making its attacks increasingly violent.
title: Thunder Serpent Narwa

value: Nergigante is a dragon with a bristly appearance. It is covered in spikes that constantly regrow as they're broken off. It has two massive horns similar to a bull. The monster's hide is primarily black with purple and orange accents. Nergigante is constantly regenerating its skin as it attacks and is struck by hunters. It will slowly grow white spikes on certain body parts, which harden over time. Hunters must try to break the white spikes before they harden. Once they have hardened, its attacks deal more damage.
title: Nergigante

value: Ruiner Nergigante has darker scales and a carapace compared to Nergigante. It features black metallic spikes between its bone spikes. Initially slow and predictable, it becomes more brutal as it takes damage. The metallic spikes grow as it loses health, causing a bigger 'spike burst' effect and inflicting Bleeding. Ruiner Nergigante is intelligent and patient, letting others weaken prey before attacking. It is fiercely aggressive and hunts with great ferocity, surpassing the strength of its normal variant.
title: Ruiner Nergigante

value: Xeno'jiiva is a large dragon with an otherworldly appearance. It has two eyes and six orange sensory organs running up the length of its horns. Parts of its body glow ethereal blue, making its skin appear crystalline and translucent. Its wings are frayed at the edge of the membrane and are grayish-blue in color. When enraged, its torso glows a hot red and orange. Xeno'jiiva wields absorbed bioenergy as bright blue flame-like energy, firing explosive bursts or concentrated beams that can cause Dragonblight or Fireblight.
title: Xeno'jiiva

value: Safi'jiiva is a large red dragon that evolved from its juvenile form Xeno'jiiva. It has rigid scales, a bent posture with chest close to the ground, and spikes from its neck to the tail. Unlike Xeno'jiiva, it has a natural appearance but can draw, manipulate, and reshape ecosystem bioenergy on a wide scale. In combat, Safi'jiiva unleashes high-power energy attacks. It also can lure other monsters and absorbs ambient energy for attacks, giving it a 'perfect being' reputation.
title: Safi'jiiva

value: Vaal Hazak is a long, slender Elder Dragon with silver scales hidden under a fleshy red veil. It has a double lower jaw with extremely long, sharp fangs. Its head features two bright yellow spots and small red eyes. Vaal Hazak has sickle-shaped claws on its toes and wingtips. It buries itself in bone piles and charges up Effluvium for powerful attacks, leaving behind Tainted Flesh. It can spew concentrated streams of Effluvium or release noxious clouds, weakening its victims. Vaal Hazak gathers Effluvium to heal itself, killing its victims in the process.
title: Vaal Hazak

value: Blackveil Vaal Hazak resembles Vaal Hazak but has green and milky-white scales and moss-like pustules. These pustules emit Effluvium and cover its head, rendering it blind. If removed, it regains sight. A passive creature, it attacks only when provoked, using Effluvium to locate prey and infect monsters. Its moss spreads, transforming the environment. A thick green fog indicates its presence. Its breath creates a smog-like gas that adheres to the ground, altering its habitat.
title: Blackveil Vaal Hazak

value: Malzeno has a dragon-like body covered in dark scales. Swarms of flying leech-like Qurio creatures cover its neck, chest, and front legs. Its wings appear dull grey on top but reveal a red underside. Each wing has a giant talon. It possesses three curved claws on its front legs and two large golden horns. Its tail ends in three movable prongs to catch prey. Malzeno can envelop its body in a swarm of Qurio for defense. It attacks using its claws, talons, and by spitting Qurio to drain life from its targets.
title: Malzeno

value: Kulve Taroth is a very large, lizard-like dragon covered in a beautiful golden mantle. Its golden scales feature lapis lazuli spiral horns, and emerald-like patches underneath. It moves slowly due to its heavy mantle but gains incredible speed when the mantle is shed. Kulve Taroth breathes superheated air and melts gold in its path, causing molten gold to rain from ceilings. Initially docile, it attacks when provoked or its mantle is broken. Fleeing occurs if hunters' damage is too low. It enters Fury Mode when enough body parts are broken, increasing its power.
title: Kulve Taroth

value: The Behemoth, from another world, is a colossal quadruped with purple skin and spiked black hair along its back. It boasts exceptional strength and the rare ability to wield magic. Its abilities include summoning windstorms, meteor showers, thunderbolts, and self-healing. It is extremely aggressive, whether innate or due to its transport to the New World. Behemoths' red-glowing eyes and bodies indicate enmity. Taming one demands a team of elite hunters equipped with top-notch gear and advanced tactics.
title: Behemoth

value: Velkhana is an Elder Dragon with a slim, upright body covered in crystalline blue scales. It has a tiara-like crown of small horns and can summon ice to cover its wings, limbs, and tail. Velkhana controls cold through ore-infused scales, breathing supercooled fluid to flash-freeze foes. It creates walls and exploding spikes of ice, and can form icy armor. Velkhana disperses freezing clouds and rains chunks of ice. It is highly agile, using its spear-like tail to strike from multiple angles. If its tail is cut off, it creates an ice prosthetic.
title: Velkhana

value: Namielle, an Elder Dragon, combines features of manta rays, deep-sea fish, and jellyfish. Its black and light blue wings have rainbow luminescence. When charged, its blue parts glow. Namielle produces watery mucus for hydration and attacks. It glides through air but moves clumsily on land until it spreads water. It uses water to slide, create shock waves, and wash away foes. Electricity-producing organs allow it to electrify water, causing violent steam explosions. Overuse of water leaves it vulnerable and dried out.
title: Namielle

value: Shara Ishvalda is a large dragon with two forms. Initially, it appears bulky, covered in rocks and minerals, with its head obscured. It lacks wings, instead having four-fingered hand-like appendages. In its true form, it is lithe and athletic, with bark-like and stone-like hide in bright colors. It manipulates earth using sonic vibrations from its fingers, creating quicksand, tunnels, tremors, and explosions. Its vibrations can collapse landmasses and alter biomes. Shara Ishvalda is nearly blind, relying on a sensory organ in its forehead. Its subterranean lifestyle and unique abilities make it a formidable monster.
title: Shara Ishvalda

value: Primordial Malzeno has a sturdy appearance with silver and gold plates and blades. It lacks Qurio and its wing membranes are dark bluish-purple. Its abilities include great physical strength and agility, enhanced by its armored wings. In its Bloodrage state, the armor blackens, and it glows magenta. If severely injured, it enters a Bloodlust state where its body turns black with orange light, and it uses its fiery form to attack fiercely. Primordial Malzeno prefers a knightly combat style with precise strikes, wielding its armor and tail skillfully.
title: Primordial Malzeno

value: Gaismagorm is a massive Elder Dragon with a pseudowyvern-like gait. It crawls using its front legs and dexterous forearms, adapted from its wings. Its tail has five spikes, and its head features two horns, beady yellow eyes, and a split mouth with hooks. Gaismagorm expels energy absorbed from Qurio through vents, enhancing its attacks. When enraged, it sheds crystallized Qurio, radiating red feather-like protrusions, and its vents erupt with flames. The Qurio swarm around it, violently protective of their host.
title: Gaismagorm

value: Basarios is the juvenile form of Gravios with a hard, mossy carapace for protection. It rarely uses its wings and prefers burrowing, blending with rocks. It releases sleeping gas, poisonous gas, and sometimes a fire plume. It hurls lava balls and uses a powerful lava beam. It charges at opponents rather than attacking from a distance.
title: Basarios

value: Gravios is a colossal wyvern covered in a dense, stone-like armored shell. It towers over most wyverns and measures several dozen feet in length. Despite having large wings, its size and weight limit its flight. Its thick tail ends in a mace-like club. Gravios expels knockout gas from gaps in its underside and flammable gas as a waste product. It inhabits volcanoes and swamps, eating rocks and minerals. Gravios is docile until provoked, fiercely territorial, and incubates its juvenile Basarios in large volcanic areas.
title: Gravios

value: Black Gravios is a common Gravios whose shell has undergone a chemical change due to an abnormally high body temperature, making it closer to a Variant classification. It is covered in a black hide with red spines and is larger and heavier than the common Gravios. Black Gravios is more proficient in the use of its heat beam, thanks to its sturdier body. Far more aggressive than its grey counterpart, it will attack on sight and commonly expels explosive gas. Black Gravios inhabits swamps, volcanoes, and even forests like the Everwood, where ore deposits are abundant.
title: Black Gravios

value: Diablos are large, bipedal wyverns with armor plating. They possess bull-like horns and tusk-like fangs, and a heavy, studded club on their tail. They can burrow underground, and their powerful legs allow high-speed charges. Known herbivores, Diablos eat desert cacti but remain aggressively territorial, battling with their horns. Though unable to sustain flight, their broad wings aid escape from sand. Diablos use a roar to stun adversaries and surprise prey with their burrowing tactic, making them a formidable creature.
title: Diablos

value: Black Diablos are female Diablos in heat, identified by their black shell during mating season. Extremely aggressive, they use the sand for ambushes and attack intruders, including other Diablos and monsters like Tigrex. Highly territorial, they are found in deserts and can tunnel through sand. Their aggression peaks during this time, making them one of the most hostile creatures in the Desert.
title: Black Diablos

value: Khezu are large, pale Wyverns with flabby, rubbery hide that they keep damp. Their blood vessels and veins show through their skin. They have specialized orifices in their tails for electrical attacks and suction pad-like toes for climbing. Their eyes have regressed, but they possess a superb sense of smell. Hermaphrodites, they reproduce by paralyzing prey and injecting their young, known as Whelps. Khezu dwell in caves, where they can cling to ceilings and ambush prey, though they venture out when food becomes scarce.
title: Khezu

value: The Red Khezu has a fatty, blood-red hide. It has greater control over its extendable neck than the common species and possesses a more potent thunder sac. The Red Khezu behaves much like an ordinary Khezu; however, it is capable of using a unique neck-thrusting attack. This subspecies, interestingly, is immune to Fire and weak to Water, unlike the Khezu. Red Khezu live generally in the same areas as normal Khezu.
title: Red Khezu

value: Monoblos are large wyverns with brown, rock-hard carapace, resembling Diablos but with a Styracosaurus-like head featuring a beaked mouth, a spiral nasal horn, and a large frill. They have small yellow eyes, spikes of various sizes, and a tail club lined with spikes. Lacking breath attacks, their roar is lethal. They tunnel in desert sands to ambush threats and fight with horn-thrusts and tail-swings. Aggressive and quick to anger, their crown gains red streaks when enraged, increasing their speed and strength.
title: Monoblos

value: Rathalos is a large, bipedal wyvern with a spiny, armored hide. Its outer shell is bright red with black markings. It possesses a flame sac for deadly flaming projectiles and poisonous talons. Its long, thick tail ends in a heavy spiked club. Rathalos is an expert flyer, hunting from the skies with surprise aerial attacks. On the ground, it uses powerful legs and its clubbed tail. Highly territorial, Rathalos chases away intruders and carries prey away to eat in private. It is the apex predator of the Ancient Forest.
title: Rathalos

value: Azure Rathalos is a subspecies of Rathalos, distinguished by its azure shell, lime green wing webbing, and a single red spike on its tail. It boasts higher stamina, health, and attack power compared to regular Rathalos. This wyvern exhibits exceptional aggression and intelligence, becoming even more fierce during mating season. It resides in forests, hills, and occasionally volcanic areas, using its powerful wings to stay airborne longer and deliver devastating fire breath and poisonous claw attacks.
title: Azure Rathalos

value: Silver Rathalos is a rare Rathalos species with a shining silver carapace and black markings along the spine. Larger than average, he activates an 'Incandescent' state where his head, chest, and neck-wings area ignite with blue flames. He frequently uses triple fireballs and aerial claw attacks, preferring aerial combat. On the ground, he relies on tail whips over bites and charges less often. Known for his rage, his attacks become even more aggressive in the Incandescent state. Hunters often find him atop ancient Towers, and he is occasionally relocated for special event hunts in Arenas.
title: Silver Rathalos

value: Rathian is a medium-sized true flying wyvern with a dull, muted green hide and a long chin spike. Her upper back and wingtips feature moss-like fur. She generates flaming projectiles and can release potent fire blasts. Her tail has sharp, poisonous needles for close combat. Rathian can run at high speeds and has keen vision. Flash Bombs are useful in combat.
title: Rathian

value: Pink Rathian has a heavily-armored body and greater health and attack power than her green relative. She often appears in quests alongside the Azure Rathalos. Her attacks are stronger and faster than those of a normal Rathian, while her other traits remain similar to the green Rathian.
title: Pink Rathian

value: Gold Rathian is a rare Rathian species with an exceptionally strong golden shell covering her body. This shell makes it difficult for Hunters to land a hit without their weapons bouncing off. She is slightly larger than the average Rathian. When she activates her 'Incandescent' state, her head, chest, and the area between her neck and wings are engulfed in blue flames. Gold Rathian is weakest to the Thunder Element and, like all Rathians, can inflict Poison ailment.
title: Gold Rathian

value: Akantors are massive wyverns dwelling in magma, covered in reddish black spikes and durable shells. They possess large sharp claws and tusks nearly as long as their head. Classified as Flying Wyverns, they lost flight capabilities and now have small wing nubs. Akantors can cause environmental damage, and their corrosive fluid and sonic roars make them extremely deadly. Highly aggressive, they attack almost anything on sight, often taking down prey with their immense strength and powerful roars.
title: Akantor

value: Nargacuga is a black-scaled wyvern resembling a predatory black panther. It has nightmarish red eyes and dark fur, suggesting nocturnal predation. Found in the Great Forest, Jungle, and Old Swamp, it uses its bladed wings and spiked tail in combat. Its tail, prehensile and flexible, can produce rattling sounds. Nargacuga can jump long distances and becomes enraged by loud noises, causing its eyes to glow and tail spikes to protrude. It rests in high, hard-to-reach trees, relying on its natural senses to detect hunters.
title: Nargacuga

value: Tigrex is a large, quadrupedal wyvern characterized by its massive head and jaws, powerful limbs, and striking yellow/blue striped coloration. It uses its powerful lungs to produce extremely loud, concussive roars. Tigrex rarely flies but excels at gliding and running at high speeds. It relies on brute strength and intimidation, flushing its skin to a bright-red glow during intense combat. Tigrex does not use elemental attacks, focusing solely on physical prowess.
title: Tigrex

value: Ukanlos, though a Flying Wyvern, lacks wings. It has a silvery-white carapace with jagged fins and a small face featuring glowing yellow eyes. Its massive, shovel-like chin flings ice and digs through frozen surfaces. Ukanlos swims through ice using its fins and can produce a devastating ice beam. Despite its size, it leaps by slamming its tail. A solitary creature, Ukanlos is fiercely territorial in remote, frozen regions like the Snowy Mountains and the Polar Field.
title: Ukanlos

value: Barioth is a quadrupedal Flying Wyvern, with a saber-toothed cat head, icy white plates, amber tusks, and spiked neck, spine, and tail. Aggressive and territorial, it attacks hunters on sight. It can slide on ice using its claws, tail-slam, homing glide from the sky, and spit an icy ball that creates a powerful tornado, inflicting Snowman Status. Inhabiting the Tundra and Snowy Mountains, Barioth preys on large herbivores, eating Popo or Anteka when fatigued.
title: Barioth

value: Sand Barioth is a large flying wyvern with an orange-yellow color scheme, a blood red head, and deep indigo tusks with blood red tips. Unlike its frosty cousins, the Sand Barioth creates dust devils to hurtle at hunters. Known for its high aggression, it soaks itself in the blood of its kills to stay cool in the desert heat. The Sand Barioth inhabits the Sandy Plains and Trese Desert.
title: Sand Barioth

value: Giggi are relatively featureless flying wyverns, spawning from large organic globules called 'Giggi Sacs' in dark caves. They have grey bodies with purple markings, small stubby arms, and round mouths with small yet sharp teeth used to drain victims' bodily fluids. Giggi produce weak poison and leech onto prey to nourish themselves and grow bigger. Though not aggressive, they are gluttonous and chase down anything they deem prey, fleeing upon exposure to light. Giggi and their cocoons can be found in various environments, from the Volcano to the Flooded Forest.
title: Giggi

value: Gigginox are blind, cave-dwelling wyverns that detect prey using body heat. Unlike Khezu, they have a flat, agile body with grey-white topskin and dark red underskin. Their elastic neck and tail can stretch twice their length. They can attach to ceilings and drag hunters down to consume them. Gigginox spit poison, release poisonous gas, and lay gelatinous globs called Giggi Sacs. When enraged, their topskin darkens. They have sharp teeth and can flip quickly. Their purple glowing eye-like markings can be broken.
title: Gigginox

value: Baleful Gigginox has a golden color on its top and dark bruise-black on its underside. Its heat-sensing organs glow green, unlike the normal Gigginox's purple. It uses Thunder and Paralysis instead of Poison, producing explosive electrical blob sacs. When enraged, its golden skin turns bloody red. Highly aggressive and territorial, it kills smaller predators near its lair. It lives mainly in the freezing caves of the Tundra.
title: Baleful Gigginox

value: The Green Nargacuga has mossy green fur, a prehensile tail ending in a bright red spike, and bright yellow eyes. It moves with advanced agility and performs a tail slam followed by a jump smash, stunning hunters if successful. Highly aggressive and bloodthirsty, it resides in the Misty Peaks, the Deserted Island, and the Flooded Forest.
title: Green Nargacuga

value: The Lucent Nargacuga, also called the Moon Swift Wyvern, has metallic azure scales, patches of white fur, and a golden chest. It can turn invisible and extend venomous spikes from its tail. Lucent Nargacuga are highly territorial and strike with cunning. They live primarily in the Tower 3 and Forlorn Arena, with occasional sightings in the Great Forest. They perform double tail slams and all tail attacks emit venomous spikes when extended, making them deadly predators under fog or night cover.
title: Lucent Nargacuga

value: Astalos is a Flying Wyvern with dark green plating and a bright-colored underside. Its wings resemble those of a Glasswing butterfly, and its tail, which is almost half its length, ends in two pairs of earwig-like pincers. Astalos generates green electricity by rubbing its body parts, releasing it through its wings, tail, and horn. Hyper-aggressive, it attacks Hunters and other monsters on sight, using dive-bombing strikes, paralyzing tail strikes, and electrical blasts. It can also breathe electricity that splits into two pillars upon impact.
title: Astalos

value: Boltreaver Astalos has a slight turquoise tint on its scales and shell. Its wing claws, tail pincers, and head crest are all much larger than those of a regular Astalos. It can supercharge itself beyond normal individuals and manipulate electromagnetism. It generates a blade-like beam of electricity from its head crest. Boltreaver Astalos are extremely aggressive, always attacking even unseen foes. Hunters require a special permit to hunt it.
title: Boltreaver Astalos

value: Ruby Basarios is a variant of Basarios with pink crystals on its back and a moss-covered body. Its shell is highly resistant to damage. It launches crystals by shaking its body. Ruby Basarios buries itself in the earth, camouflaging as a rock. It attacks if disturbed but remains passive when approached while buried.
title: Ruby Basarios

value: Bloodbath Diabolos, after losing one of its horns, developed a new warped horn, while its remaining horn grew extremely large. Blue blood covers its face, wings, and tail. It wields greater strength and aggression, charging foes at incredible speeds despite its size. Angered, it displays flame-shaped veins on its body and releases steam that causes explosions due to its elevated body temperature, enabling it to change direction swiftly during charges.
title: Bloodbath Diabolos

value: Silverwind Nargacuga resembles a normal Nargacuga but features white fur and scales in bright striped patterns. It possesses more spikes on its tail that protrude constantly and often grows larger than typical Nargacuga. Faster and stronger, it expertly throws tail spines. Notably, Silverwind Nargacuga generates powerful shockwaves with fast tail swings, cutting through opponents' defenses and causing deep gashes. Unlike regular Nargacuga, it attacks from a distance and displays heightened aggression.
title: Silverwind Nargacuga

value: The Dreadking Rathalos bears a strong resemblance to normal Rathalos, but is larger with thicker talons and tail spines. Its crimson scales and iridescent black plating set it apart. The wings are a darker brown with cream-colored markings. Dreadking Rathalos shoot fireballs that drain hunter health, and their venomous talons contain Noxious Poison. They can fire three fireballs at a time, like their female counterparts.
title: Dreadking Rathalos

value: Dreadqueen Rathian are larger than normal Rathian, with purple-tinted scales that secrete poison. They possess the same moveset as relatives but also use their poisonous tails to inflict various levels of poison. They combine flame bites and tail attacks for increased danger. When enraged, they fly high and plummet down, striking targets with their tails. Ultra-aggressive and territorial, hunting them requires a special permit.
title: Dreadqueen Rathian

value: Seregios is a medium-sized bipedal wyvern covered in lustrous gold scales. Its face features red markings around the eyes which continue along its body, its head being topped with a single, backwards-curving horn. Its feet are zygodactyl in nature, with two toes facing forward and two toes facing backward. Each of Seregios's scales can swivel individually as it will use them to perform a rattling display when agitated. Seregios can fire its quill-like scales at attackers as well as using its powerful feet and talons to slash and grasp prey and foes alike.
title: Seregios

value: Grimclaw Tigrex have blue forelimbs and heads with pulsating veins that turn red when enraged. They throw large boulders and snowballs, and produce explosive roars unexpectedly. Unlike Brute Tigrex, they can sharply turn and throw projectiles after missing a charge. Their evolved forelimbs allow for more powerful attacks.
title: Grimclaw Tigrex

value: Molten Tigrex features striking blood red coloration interspersed with bright blue spines and claws. Slightly larger than other Tigrex, it creates explosive blasts while charging, spinning, and biting. Its attacks leave a target with explosive powder that detonates over time. It has two rage modes: the first increases speed and enables the Brute Tigrex's stampede attack, while the second state involves explosive roar, visible veins, and lunge spin attack. Highly aggressive, it fiercely defends its territory.
title: Molten Tigrex

value: Frostfang Barioth is a formidable flying wyvern with tusks that are dark at the base and icy white at the front. Its fur is thick and aged-looking, especially on its forelimbs and face. The monster's body is covered in frost, reflecting its ability to breathe sub-zero air. This breath leaves frost trails that slow hunters. It also expels explosive ice projectiles and retains its physical strength, dealing heavy damage with its icy breath and powerful movements.
title: Frostfang Barioth

value: Bazelgeuse is a large Flying Wyvern with thick, heavy scales that shine bronze. Its bulky upper body and large wingspan make it a formidable foe. Bazelgeuse uses its scales to attack, dropping them like landmines that explode upon contact. In its superheated state, scales detonate seconds after falling. It releases fire blasts, pierces with loud roars, and tackles foes with its sturdy build. Dominating its territory, Bazelgeuse fights any intruders, seeking battles and engaging in turf wars with Deviljho, which often result in dangerous ties.
title: Bazelgeuse

value: Seething Bazelgeuse, a variant of Bazelgeuse, features a metallic blue body in its normal state, akin to its heated state. It powers up further with a white-hot head and tail, then sears to purple-white. Explosive scales in its first powered state (Seething State) are flung over wide ranges, while its second state (Crimson Lotus State) causes scales to glow purple-hot, sticking to surfaces before huge explosions. It ends with a massive aerial scale-scattering maneuver followed by a ground crash, depleting its scales and reverting to its normal state.
title: Seething Bazelgeuse

value: Apex Diablos exhibits a bloodier color scheme than its kin, with larger horns and vivid red eyes. Scars glow brighter when enraged. It is brutal and attacks relentlessly, utilizing powerful corkscrew burrows and multi-charges. When fully enraged, it performs devastating charges. It surpasses Bloodbath Diablos in ferocity, constantly in a state of extreme rage until a second phase of increased fury. Apex Diablos's combat prowess and aggressive nature make it a formidable flying wyvern adversary.
title: Apex Diablos

value: Legiana is a slim, beige and navy-blue Flying Wyvern with leaf-like wingtips and striped skin patterns. It has antenna-like fins attached to its neck via a thin membrane, enabling extreme speeds and chilling winds. Its bright yellow eyes and powerful legs with piercing talons make it a formidable hunter. Legiana is intensely territorial, attacking intruders in the upper reaches of the Coral Highlands. It can emit chilling winds, fly with precision, and grab prey out of the air with its strong talons.
title: Legiana

value: Shrieking Legiana, structurally similar to regular Legiana, possess black coloration and ice-encrusted spikes on their chest, tail, and wings. They excel in manipulating ice, creating large ice spikes during attacks. They can execute powerful dive attacks. Their adaptation to the Hoarfrost Reach enhances their abilities, making them formidable in cold climates.
title: Shrieking Legiana

value: Paolumu is a bat-like wyvern covered in white fur with a pink face and brown scales on its back, underbelly, and tail. It has rodent-like buck teeth and an air sac in its neck that inflates to allow it to float and attack from above. Paolumu uses its tail to batter foes and breathes wind to draw in hunters. It is calm unless provoked and can be deflated by mounting or attacking its neck. Paolumu is outclassed by Deviljho, Legiana, and Odogaron in the Coral Highlands.
title: Paolumu

value: Nightshade Paolumu is a large Flying Wyvern resembling a bat. Its body is covered in soft, dark fur, contrasting with its pale white face and brightly colored neck patches. It inflates with a single inhalation and flies at high speeds in unpredictable patterns. It expels a pale blue-green gas that acts as a powerful narcotic, putting hunters to sleep. It can manipulate this gas using wind currents from its tail, making it a formidable opponent in aerial combat.
title: Nightshade Paolumu

value: The Apex Rathalos, akin to Dreadking Rathalos in size but bearing normal-ish skin tone and numerous red scars, stands out for its orange-glowing neck when firing. Hyperviolent and extremely aggressive, it swoops with enlarged, delayed-explosion fireballs, unlike typical Rathalos. Claw attacks launch hunters airborne for targeted fireballs, and in fury, it bombards areas from the sky. Its ability to send out slower but massive, explosive fireballs makes it particularly dangerous. It signals attacks and roars by the orange light emanating from its neck.
title: Apex Rathalos

value: Apex Rathian, a variant of Rathian, features red scars and glowing red eyes. Her sharp teeth, spiked wings, and large tail spines distinguish her. Brutal and aggressive, she attacks on sight. Her abilities include impaling poisonous spikes, diagonal tail strikes, and an ear-piercing roar. When enraged, she performs a triple tail swipe, spreading multiple spikes. Apex Rathian's behavior mirrors her male counterpart, with enhanced ferocity and a powerful venomous cloud from her spikes.
title: Apex Rathian

value: Rey Dau is a large bipedal wyvern with golden bony scales and smaller black scales on its underbelly, joints, and wings. Its wings are a single membrane with bladed protrusions. It has a short, serpentine head with two rows of teeth and mandible-like protrusions. Its horns can channel electricity like a rail gun. During thunderstorms, it harnesses lightning to create powerful blasts. Its wing blades and back spikes glow bright blue when charged. It can also crystallize sand into sharp glass blades, enhancing its attacks.
title: Rey Dau

value: Gendrome, a Bird wyvern, exhibits a prominent crest and a slightly larger size compared to Genprey. This crest signifies leadership within the pack. Gendrome possess paralyzing fangs and are notably faster and stronger than Velocidrome, posing a significant challenge to novice hunters. Their responsibilities include choosing prey, leading hunts, and patrolling their territory alongside Genprey.
title: Gendrome

value: Genprey are Bird Wyverns with vibrant yellow and green hides and distinctive yellow crests over their eyes. They leap great distances and use their fangs to paralyze foes. Genprey follow a hierarchical structure led by a Gendrome, acting aggressively towards intruders and rallying to attack larger threats in unison.
title: Genprey

value: Giaprey are agile Bird wyverns with crystal blue scales and sky blue stripes. They hunt in packs and are stronger than Velociprey. In battle, Giaprey spit ice to freeze enemies. They often join White Velociprey packs, enhancing their overall combat prowess. Despite their ice-spitting ability, Giaprey are not significantly more dangerous than Velociprey.
title: Giaprey

value: Gypceros is among the largest and heaviest known Bird Wyverns. It has a lamp-like crest on top of its head and a rubbery, shock-resistant hide. Its long pink tail has a bulbous tip, and it is a uniform blue-grey across most of its body. It possesses a poison sac to produce toxic fluids. Gypceros can disorient foes with its crest, use its elastic tail as a whip, and spit large globules of poison. Despite being naturally timid, it is intelligent and feigns death to surprise attackers.
title: Gypceros

value: Purple Gypceros possess a vivid purple and green plumage, unlike their blue and pink counterparts. These bird wyverns show increased resilience and can produce potent poison. In battle, they frequently use flashing attacks and seamlessly chain other strikes. They inhabit swamps, marshlands, and occasionally jungles, where they hunt for insects and mushrooms. Their love for warm and humid environments makes them well-adapted to such locations, and their adaptability allows them to thrive where other species struggle.
title: Purple Gypceros

value: Iodrome is significantly larger than normal Ioprey and possesses a larger crest. Unlike other pack leaders, Iodrome has a more amphibian look, with salamander-like skin that glistens with moisture, and a frog-like throat sac. It lacks sharp claws, relying on poison to bring down prey like Aptonoth and enemies. Iodrome can quickly cripple hunters with its pack and poison, thanks to its speed, agility, and considerable strength. When threatened, it spits poison from the poison sac in its throat. Iodrome leads a pack of Ioprey and always travels with them for protection.
title: Iodrome

value: Ioprey have lithe, raptor-esque bodies with vivid red skin, black markings, and purplish lavender coloration by the limbs. They possess large, rounded head crests and lower jaws that curl outwards. Ioprey are notable for their bulbous frog-like throat sacs, which house potent poison sacs. They spit toxins at foes, and their pack hunting behavior makes them dangerous. The duration and toxicity of their poison vary by rank, with G-Rank Ioprey being particularly hazardous.
title: Ioprey

value: Velocidrome, a large Bird Wyvern, stands out with its bright red crest over blue hide, signifying leadership among Velociprey. Despite its weak physical strength, it leads packs with strategic intelligence. Velocidrome often employs a dual-leader system to enhance hunting efficiency, allowing the pack to split into two groups. This unique approach ensures successful hunts and solidifies its position as a respected leader.
title: Velocidrome

value: Velociprey are bird wyverns with blue scales featuring black stripes and a dull red crest on their heads. Their yellow eyes and white belly give them a distinct appearance. Traveling in packs, they inhabit various environments and are known for their swift movements and quick attacks. Experienced hunters find them manageable, but new hunters struggle due to their great leaping distance and relentless assaults. Velociprey's ability to deliver eggs and large ores makes fleeing challenging.
title: Velociprey

value: Scarred Yian Garuga is nearly identical to Yian Garuga but has a missing ear and a scar across its face. It is only found in a tempered state. Known for its extreme aggression, it exhibits no change in behavior from its unscarred counterpart. Its missing ear and facial scar are thought to be battle wounds, indicating a history of intense combat. Hunters must be prepared for relentless attacks when encountering this tempered bird wyvern.
title: Scarred Yian Garuga

value: Yian Kut-Ku possess large, fan-like ear frills that unfurl when excited, and an enlarged lower bill. They have a large, yellow beak with a pink stripe running down the upper beak. Their bodies are covered in orange-pink plating and beige scales. With deep blue wing membranes and bright yellow eyes, these small wyverns are quick and agile. Known for their powerful hearing, they can move swiftly and shoot flaming projectiles from their flame sacs. They are temperamental, skittish, and inquisitive, often spreading their wings and squawking loudly to appear bigger.
title: Yian Kut-Ku

value: Blue Yian Kut-Ku are smaller and stronger than pink Yian Kut-Ku, with an indigo carapace and blue streaks on their beaks and ears. They shoot flaming projectiles and move quickly. They are cautious, easily startled by loud noises. Despite their aggression, they remain wary and prefer to avoid confrontation unless provoked.
title: Blue Yian Kut-Ku

value: Giadrome is physically similar to Velocidrome, with pale blue coloration and a frost sac. It possesses more powerful attack power and higher defense than ordinary Giaprey. Giadrome can spit a frozen substance to immobilize prey and foes. Territorial and traveling in packs, it coordinates attacks using barks and calls. Giadrome's frost sac allows it to thrive in cold climates, making it a formidable hunter in icy regions.
title: Giadrome

value: Hypnocatrice is a medium-sized Bird Wyvern covered in bright, colorful feathers. It has extremely powerful legs with large feet, and a hard, sharp beak. Despite its feathered appearance, Hypnocatrice flies using pterosaur or bat-like membraned wings, leaving its feather arrangements strictly for display purposes. It possesses a sleep sac, which allows it to produce a colorless mist that can cause attackers to fall unconscious.
title: Hypnocatrice

value: Yian Garuga boasts striking purple coloration, large defensive spikes, and an extremely tough shell. Its beak is sharp and jagged, and it has a silver mane around the edge of its face. Its body resembles that of a Yian Kut-Ku. Yian Garuga wields a poisonous tail club and emits ear-splitting roars to immobilize foes. Highly aggressive, it avoids pitfall traps and remains unaffected by sonic bombs. Its behavior is similar to a Rathian, with a shared set of attacks.
title: Yian Garuga

value: Baggi resemble small theropod dinosaurs called dromaeosaurs. Their hide is primarily blue, with a grayish underbelly and a streak of yellow between. Patches of black fur can be found on the back of the head, above the shoulders, and especially the tail. Baggi can put other creatures to sleep using a tranquilizing fluid, lasting only a few seconds. Unlike their Jaggi cousins, they attack without delay, rushing at hunters without warning.
title: Baggi

value: Gargwa are flightless birds resembling the extinct Dodo, with small vestigial wings. Docile herbivores, they attack and flee when threatened, often leaving eggs or guano. Coexisting with Yukumo villagers as livestock, they occasionally attack hunters. Known for their distinctive calls and bulky bodies, Gargwa play a unique role in local ecosystems.
title: Gargwa

value: Great Baggi is a mature male Baggi who leads its pack. It appears slightly larger than the Great Jaggi and possesses a distinctive head crest. Notably, it can spit a tranquilizing liquid to induce sleep in prey and hunters. Additionally, it can command its Baggi to encircle and assault targets with a coordinated tranquilizer attack. In encounters with other boss monsters, Great Baggi often prioritizes fighting them, temporarily ceasing to engage with hunters.
title: Great baggi

value: The Great Jaggi stands out with its lavender scales, crimson and tangerine frills, and furs along its neck and back. Its muzzle is thin and wrinkly, while its tail barbs are blunt. It often attacks other monsters, showing competitiveness and intelligence. It can call its Jaggi pack with a loud roar and exhibits boss-like traits, such as becoming enraged and limping when weak. The Great Jaggi can also be summoned by Qurupeco in the Deserted Island, Sandy Plains, and Misty Peaks.
title: Great jaggi

value: The Great Wroggi has slick salamander-like skin similar to the Iodrome. It is usually accompanied by a group of smaller Wroggi. Its most prominent feature is a delicate, translucent sac-like organ around the neck. This sac contains poison, visible before it spews out as a toxic mist. Great Wroggi use this ability to defend themselves and attack hunters. They are known for their cunning and pack tactics, making them formidable opponents in the wild.
title: Great wroggi

value: Jaggi are pink bird wyverns with purple foreclaws, back-stripe, and head-frills, and cream-colored underbellies. Their tails are barbed, and they have sizable frills behind their jaws. Jaggi cooperate more than other raptors but are easily dealt with due to their boisterous nature. They spend time vocalizing bark-like warnings and attacking intruders. Novice hunters often encounter them as their first challenge.
title: Jaggi

value: Jaggia, the females of the Jaggi species, display a blue-purple hue with orange limbs and cream-colored underbellies. Unlike males, they have soft, feather-like tails and subdued frills. They exhibit higher cooperation and greater resilience, making them a challenge for hunters. They spend time 'barking' at threats and occasionally sleep. Jaggia are larger and bulkier, with a more substantial area of attack, making them formidable in packs.
title: Jaggia

value: Qurupeco has three digits on each foot with partial webbing. It masters vocal mimicry to summon, heal, and boost other monsters. Its crest and throat sac are delicate, making it susceptible to loud noises. Its beak and wingtips can break for rewards like Strange Beak and Flintstone. Capable of flight, it avoids traps and uses Sonic Bombs for disorientation, causing it to drop items when interrupted.
title: Qurupeco

value: The Crimson Qurupeco, unlike its normal kin, sports vibrant red and blue feathers. It uses electric flints in its wing talons to create a shock capable of inflicting Thunderblight. It can blind hunters with a bright flash similar to Gypceros. Its roar is loud enough to require Earplugs. This subspecies's vocal sac only appears during vocalizations. Its song can either heal or boost nearby monsters but can be turned against them if interrupted. One unique song sends summoned monsters into instant Rage mode.
title: Crimson Qurupeco

value: Wroggi are small, orange Bird Wyverns with slick, water-resistant hides. They possess poison sacs on either side of their heads, which they use to weaken enemies. Their movements and body structure are similar to Jaggia. Though poisonous, they are not as strong or resistant to poison as their leader. Wroggi often travel in packs and coordinate their attacks using high-pitched calls.
title: Wroggi

value: Great Maccao's feathered crest sets them apart from its Maccao pack. Although superficially similar to its younger brethren, a Great Maccao is bigger and bulkier, with more brightly-colored plumage. It also has powerful legs it uses to kick threats and to move with great speed, and its spiked tail allows it to propel itself forward and jump with tremendous force. Great Maccao has little control over its pack and prefers to run rather than fight, but it works with its pack to hunt large prey.
title: Great Maccao

value: Malfestio, resembling an owl, has blue plumage, a grayish belly, yellow collar, and white face with red eyes. Its head features long, yellow-tipped tufts, and its legs end in scaly feet with two talons and a small toe. Its wings have bright blue membranes and blade-like claws. Malfestio produces yellow scales to disorient prey and an ultrasound beam to induce sleep. Nocturnal, it attacks with talons and wing claws, especially when enraged. It sleeps in trees to avoid predators.
title: Malfestio

value: Deadeye Yian Garuga has one glowing red eye that leaves a red contrail. One ear is clipped, and its wing membranes and tail tip are covered in green moss. Unlike other Deviants, Deadeye is smaller than a normal Yian Garuga. Its unique appearance and abilities make it a notable variant.
title: Deadeye Yian Garuga

value: Aknosom, a crane-like Bird Wyvern, boasts a red and gold parasol-like collar around its head, matching its wing and head-crest colors. With a long beak, yellow eyes, white scales, and feathered patches, it intimidates foes using its crest and collar. Standing on one leg, it uses its beak to stab the ground and its wings for swipes. It spits fireballs that leave burning patches and rushes at high speeds. Known for territorial behavior, Aknosom defends its territory fiercely with its versatile collar and aerial attacks.
title: Aknosom

value: Boggi, resembling small theropod dinosaurs, have yellow scaly hides with brown markings. Their hyena-like heads feature large ears, oversized canines, and dark hair on their shoulders and tails. Sporting gold eyes, they hunt in packs using sharp claws and swift movements. As low-ranking prey, they evade predators like Garangolm and Gore Magala. Despite their cunning and patience, Boggis will flee when danger arises.
title: Boggi

value: The Great Izuchi, covered in short orange fur and cream-colored scales, sports cobra-like fangs and sharp, bony scythe tails. Intelligent and cooperative, it commands its two sharpest pack members for coordinated attacks. Agile, it flips and swings with ease. Capable of regurgitating rocks at foes from great distances, it always travels in a group of three or more. This Bird Wyvern's tail can slice through bamboo stalks with a single strike, making it a formidable hunter and protector.
title: Great Izuchi

value: Kulu-Ya-Ku resembles raptorial Bird Wyverns like Velocidrome, with light brown scales and head and arm feathers. Its dodo-like face and spiked tongue help prevent yolk spills during egg-eating. Using small boulders for defense or tossing them at foes, they often steal Rathian eggs. Hunters can remove rocks with Slinger Ammos or break them with multiple strikes. In Master Rank, they use ore chunks for stronger impact or flint rocks that explode on contact. Their head and arms can be wounded.
title: Kulu-Ya-Ku

value: Pukei-Pukei, with its green feather-like scales, vibrant neck and wing feathers, and limited color-shifting capability, stores properties of its food in its mouth and tail to create poison with various effects. It bullies weaker monsters and cowers before stronger ones. When enraged, its head and wing feathers turn red. Fatigue causes feather color to fade and eyes to narrow. Hunters can manipulate its poison by controlling its diet.
title: Pukei-Pukei

value: Coral Pukei-Pukei retains most of its characteristics from Pukei-Pukei. However, Coral Pukei-Pukei sports bright orange coloring on its face, feet, and front wing claws. These turn red when it becomes enraged. The rest of its body has a pinkish-red tint to it while its underbelly and tail tip are violet. Coral Pukei-Pukei uses water as its main element of attack, storing high concentrations in its tail by drinking sponge-like plants in the Coral Highlands. It shoots jets of high pressurized water beams and throws water balls from its mouth, showing more aggression than Pukei-Pukei.
title: Coral Pukei-Pukei

value: Tzitzi-Ya-Ku is a medium-sized, navy-blue Deinonychus-like Bird Wyvern with powerful hind legs and retractable, vivid frills. It disorients foes using head flash attacks, often kicking before retreating. Living in Coral Highlands undergrowth, it uses darkness to hunt, preys on Shamos, and flashes monsters randomly to flee. Although low on the food chain, it exploits weaknesses and stuns rivals with flashes, including interrupting hunter-monster battles. Tzitzi-Ya-Ku often targets vulnerable eyes, showcasing both cunning and agility in survival strategies.
title: Tzitzi-Ya-Ku

value: Barroth is a large, bipedal monster with a crown structure atop its skull housing its nostrils. It has rigid plates covering its body for desert protection. Barroth uses its heavy head to plow through the environment and damage living things. It can submerge itself in cool mud during scorching desert days. Barroth rolls in dirt and mud to cool off and becomes extremely territorial when disturbed, releasing a warning roar and continuous attacks if provoked.
title: Barroth

value: Jade Barroth, known as Ice Crushing Wyverns, cover themselves in snow for protection. Their crown is larger than their desert cousin's. They perform U-turn charges through snow, head slams, and shake off snow to inflict Snowman on hunters. Temperamental, they prefer a wide berth and remain docile unless provoked. Angered, they relentlessly charge, bite, tail whip, and stomp until their targets are dead.
title: Jade Barroth

value: Brachydios features a glowing, green slime-covered horn and 'fists,' along with shiny, armored hide covered in obsidian plates. It wields long, developed forelimbs for defense. Its horn and fists emit explosive slime. Brachydios leaps with powerful hind legs and withstands its own blasts. When enraged, its fists, horn, and ridges glow yellow and orange, triggering instant explosions upon impact. Extremely territorial, it aggressively drives other monsters out.
title: Brachydios

value: Deviljho is a massive, bipedal Brute Wyvern, characterized by forest green color and muscular upper body. Its hide features short jagged spines, especially prominent on its back and tail. Deviljho has a narrow snout and powerful jaws, capable of delivering bone-crunching bites and throwing large objects. It can run swiftly with its massive hind legs and use its head as a battering weapon. Deviljho, when enraged, produces a dragon-element emission, which can be deadly. Nomadic and perpetually hungry, it seeks sustenance, exhibiting cannibalism and eating prey alive.
title: Deviljho

value: The Savage Deviljho remains in a rage state, driven by constant starvation. Its body emits a dark red Dragon Element haze, its eyes turn blood-red, and its scales are darker. Capable of complex Dragon Element breath attacks, it is faster and stronger than standard Deviljhos. Its hunger leads it to attack anything and depopulate entire ecosystems indiscriminately, making it one of the most brutal and aggressive creatures in its domain.
title: Savage Deviljho

value: Duramboros is a bulky, heavyset, bovine monster with stocky, muscular legs and a thick upper body. It features a very thick outer hide that is riddled with heavy folds, with a row of armored plates running along its back and tail. Duramboros' most distinctive features include its massive tail club, twin brow horns, and a pair of energy-rich dorsal humps. Duramboros' slow lifestyle often results in a layer of moss and algae growing upon its hide, most noticeably along the back.
title: Duramboros

value: Rust Duramboros lives in the Sandy Plains, where its hide has turned copper-pink from sand and grit. Unlike common Duramboros, it has a wide, axe-like tail club. Retaining its juvenile appearance due to the lack of moss and algae, it stores fat reserves in its large back humps and thick tail. This herbivore can survive months without food or water, adapting to the harsh desert environment.
title: Rust Duramboros

value: Uragaan has a gold-colored hide covered in hard crystals on its back and a rock-like chin. It can roll its body for speed, and its sticky underbelly helps attach explosive rocks. It attacks by jaw slams and tail sweeps. It produces sleep gas or flames depending on its color, which causes sleep or fireblight. Found primarily in the Volcano region and occasionally in the temperate Deserted Island or the Elder's Recess.
title: Uragaan

value: Steel Uragaan possesses a deep metallic blue hide, likely due to its diet of unique ores. It can inflict Soiled status using sulfuric yellow gas and throws burning rocks that release soiling gas upon explosion. It primarily inhabits the Volcano but occasionally ventures to the Deserted Island.
title: Steel Uragaan

value: Raging Brachydios, larger than regular Brachydios, moves slower due to its size. It sports a green shell covered in yellow-orange slime mold on its forelimbs, head, back, and tail that turns red when enraged. The slime mold is highly volatile and explodes faster than normal Brachydios slime. When enraged, Raging Brachydios loses slime, replacing it with molten lava-like substance that it spreads and detonates. This state makes its damaged shell vulnerable to attacks.
title: Raging Brachydios

value: Glavenus is a large Brute Wyvern with dark red scales and two rows of blue, bony plates along his back. His underside is cream-colored, and he has a sword-like tail that turns rusty or fiery red during battle. Glavenus is relentless and aggressive, using his powerful hind legs and sharp tail to attack. He can sharpen his tail by scraping off soot and ores with his mouth, increasing its attack power and creating explosions. Glavenus is considered one of the strongest and most intelligent Brute Wyverns.
title: Glavenus

value: Hellblade Glavenus are all male, and they resemble an enraged Glavenus. They have deep red skin, large blood-red horns and dorsal spines, and their throat-situated flame sacs and bladelike tails glow with heat or have a magma-like texture. Hellblade Glavenus produce highly explosive powder that they use to enhance their spit, coat their tails, and emit with certain attacks. They inhabit jungles, deserts, and volcanic areas.
title: Hellblade Glavenus

value: Crystalbeard Uragaan has an orange-red body, blending well in volcanic habitats. It features shiny, blue-ish purple minerals protruding from its tail and chin. This gem-clad Deviant throws crystal-like rocks from its tail, releasing sleep gas. It flings rocks at enemies with its chin. Crystalbeard Uragaan only live in volcanic environments and require a special permit to hunt.
title: Crystalbeard Uragaan

value: Anjanath has black and grey fur on its back, pink hide, and flesh-toned scales. It has large fangs, a bulky tail with quills, and small forearms. Anjanath uses its powerful jaws to lift and throw prey, and its flame sac to breathe fire. It marks territory with mucus from its nasal crest. Relentless predators, Anjanath chase down attackers and reveal dorsal sails when threatened. Vulnerable areas include its head, legs, and tail, which can be severed.
title: Anjanath

value: Fulgur Anjanath is a charged-up variant of Anjanath, with a bright blue light illuminating its nasal crest and mouth. It uses powerful jaws and legs to strike foes, and generates electric energy with a special organ. When charged, its mucus becomes electrified, allowing it to hurl it at opponents. Fulgur Anjanath can hold its own against top-tier predators like Barioth or Tigrex, and overpower monsters like Viper Tobi-Kadachi.
title: Fulgur Anjanath

value: Banbaro is a large and heavy-set Brute Wyvern. Most of its body is covered in stout white fur, with portions on its head, legs, and arms being covered in gray scales. Its giant horns are its defining characteristic. These stout, pinkish horns end in broad, moose-like antlers. When enraged, a small horn-like projection on its snout folds out. Banbaro is normally a fairly docile herbivore, but it is territorial and can be aggressive when provoked. In battle, Banbaro charges opponents while digging up soil or fallen trees, using them as projectiles or to create hindering debris piles.
title: Banbaro

value: Acidic Glavenus is a male-only species with an emerald and blue body, dark grey spikes, and horns. Its tail, covered in yellow crystals, reveals a dull blue blade underneath. With dark red eyes, it uses precise tail slashes and jabs. The acidic crystals on its tail cause Defense Down on contact. When the crystals are scraped off, its tail becomes lighter, allowing for more agile movements. Acidic Glavenus switches between heavy, powerful strikes and swift, precise attacks based on its tail's condition.
title: Acidic Glavenus

value: Radobaan resembles Uragaan with a stocky frame, enormous chin, and brow horns. It wears bones secured with sticky tar secreted through its skin, typically with spiny vertebrae around its chin and Diablos horns on its haunches. It rolls at high speeds, dispenses a sleep-inducing agent, and uses its chin for tremors. It throws heavy bones from its hide as projectiles. Despite being smaller than Uragaan, it moves more swiftly but easily loses balance while rolling. When toppled, its body can be mined for resources. It regains armor by digging through bones.
title: Radobaan

value: Quematrice, a Brute Wyvern, boasts green scales with red and blue stripes, short arms, and large hind legs. Its head features reddish orange crests, a large comb, and a throat wattle. Quematrice's tail emits flammable oil that ignites with friction, allowing it to create waves of fire. It fights aggressively and competes for carrion.
