Known for its volatile tail oil, Quematrice can both scorch enemies and set environments ablaze. This unique trait makes it a formidable opponent.
title: Quematrice

value: Rompopolo is a bizarre brute wyvern with a sharp beak and long tongue that forks at the end. It has mantis-like claws and gecko toe pads. Its dark blue and purple skin contains expandable sacs of poisonous gas. It attacks with sweeping breaths of poison mist and lashing its tongue. The tail stinger can cause explosive ground damage. Rompopolo uses its spiracles to vent gas and has beady reddish violet eyes. Its primary goal is territorial defense, making it a dangerous foe to those who enter its domain.
title: Rompopolo

value: Starving Deviljho differs from its counterparts with its bright gold color, red glowing parts, and blade-like spikes along its back, tail, and jaw. Its Dragon elemental energy causes its spikes to turn blood-red. Enraged, it shoots Dragon energy balls, and its stomp attack triggers a Dragon burst. It throws three boulders normally, but a single giant boulder when enraged. Found in the Arena and Historical Site, Starving Deviljho feeds voraciously, behaving similarly to Savage Deviljho.
title: Starving Deviljho

value: Disaster Wyvern, a unique Brute Wyvern, boasts a Brachydios-like body with blade-like arms and claws. Its sharp face features a front horn and a long red horn at the back of its head. Its tail has red-tipped lime green fin-like spikes and fins. Its legs have three red claws and red spikes behind them. Most of its scales are lime green, and its underbelly is grey with downward spikes. Disaster Wyvern can charge with incredible speed, make sharp turns, and punch hunters with enhanced steam power. In Rage Mode, it releases excess steam to increase its speed and inflict waterblight with its tail.
title: Disaster Wyvern

value: The Blango is a white-furred, ape-like creature with a bright red face. It has dull purple skin beneath its fur and combines mandrill and Japanese macaque features. Blangos burrow through snow and stalk on rocks. They hunt in packs and show increased ferocity under the lead of a Blangonga, who triggers them to throw ice chunks causing the Snowman status when enraged. Weak alone, they gain formidable strength in packs. Their habitat often includes snow and icy terrain.
title: Blango

value: The Blangonga is twice as large as the average Blango with a much more aggressive temperament. Its strong, muscled limbs allow it to run and leap at a startling speed, and its powerful roar can catch hunters unaware without adequate hearing protection. Blangongas can summon Blangos to fight beside them, perform damaging dives, break ice to hurl at hunters, and create ice shrapnel. They are incredibly aggressive but shy away from anything larger than themselves. They live in cold habitats, where their thick coat keeps them warm and serves as camouflage.
title: Blangonga

value: Copper Blangonga stands out with its brown fur instead of the typical white coat. Its face features a larger set of canines. Unlike regular Blangonga, it throws giant rocks and uses a sand breath attack to instantly knock out hunters and Felyne Comrades. Growing larger and stronger due to the loss of its troop, Copper Blangonga poses a significant threat.
title: Copper Blangonga

value: Bulldromes have larger tusks than Bullfango, and a layer of white fur, as opposed to Bullfango's black fur. Bulldromes are more agile when running and proficient at finding and locking onto their victims. They have excellent tracking skills, and can be very aggressive towards their accompanying pack of Bullfango when hungry.
title: Bulldrome

value: Congalalas resemble giant, tailed gorillas with pink fur and hippo-like heads. They have long front claws and a spike of hair on their head, which signifies the pack leader. Despite their cute appearance, Congalalas are intelligent and surprisingly aggressive when threatened. They remain docile around herbivores but react violently to hunters. When injured, they enter rage mode, turning red and releasing fart gas, significantly increasing their aggression.
title: Congalala

value: Emerald Congalala has green and white coloration, a large beard, and a taller hair spike. It produces greater amounts of gas and farts more often than normal Congalala. When it eats the object its tail holds, it breathes out dangerous stomach fluids. Consuming mushrooms causes poison, sleep, paralysis, or flames. Otherwise, the breath mimics its farts. Curious yet aggressive, Emerald Congalala rarely appears with young Congas.
title: Emerald Congalala

value: Rajang, a black ape-like beast with crimson eyes and gold horns, exhibits extreme violence and territoriality. It uses physical strength and agility in combat, and can shoot electricity. In Rage Mode, its front fur rises and turns gold. In Rampage Mode, its speed and power increase, and its forelimbs become red and nearly indestructible. This mode can be halted by damaging its tail or after a time limit.
title: Rajang

value: Furious Rajang is an extremely powerful Rajang that has lost its tail, allowing it to fully harness its abilities. Its fur stands up, and it emits an electric aura during Rage mode. This fanged beast channels raw energy into devastating attacks, becoming nearly unstoppable when enraged.
title: Furious Rajang

value: Arzuros has turquoise fur and an ursine body structure. Its back features tough hide, somewhat like a carapace. It has large, bulky legs connected to smaller feet and a short, wide tail. Armed with elongated red claws and tough braced forearms, Arzuros uses its body and agility for charge attacks and pinning prey. Omnivorous, it enjoys fish and honey, often becoming distracted while eating honey. When threatened, Arzuros uses size and claws to intimidate or employs agility to escape, frequently hanging its tongue out of its mouth.
title: Arzuros

value: Lagombi resembles a mix between a wombat, a bear, and a rabbit. Its ears are long and highly sensitive, and its face features a beak-like mouth along with small red eyes. Lagombi's belly is made of a low-friction material and is shaped so that it can slide upon the ice like a sled. It is covered in a thick fur coat to keep it warm in its sub-zero homeland. Lagombi attacks by throwing chunks of ice, swiping with its claws, and sliding around on its belly to ram foes.
title: Lagombi

value: Volvidon is a medium-sized monster, slightly larger than an Aptonoth. Covered in segmented red armor plates, it can curl into a ball for defense and offense. Equipped with a long, sticky chameleon-like tongue, Volvidon spits paralyzing liquid and releases a foul odor. As an insectivore, it hunts Neopterons, and can be aggressive when cornered, pulling foes toward its mouth or crushing them with a ball attack. Generally, Volvidon avoids trouble, making it a surprisingly formidable opponent in combat.
title: Volvidon

value: Redhelm Arzuros are among the largest of their species. Their forearms, feet, eyes, and upper body-fur are blood-red in color. Aside from those sections, their bodies are primarily blue, with their carapace and lower body-fur being a greenish blue. A pronounced strip of fur atop their head gives the impression of a mohawk. Redhelm Arzuros use their increased size and strength to their advantage during battle. They are known to leap high into the air, landing with incredible force in an attempt to crush an opponent. They also use their larger claws and more powerful arms to rip through the ground and hurl debris through the air, and have even been observed sending entire schools of fish flying by performing this action in watery environments.
title: Redhelm Arzuros

value: Gammoth is the largest known Fanged Beast, resembling a Woolly Mammoth. She has bluish fur with white, red, and brown plating. Her trunk breathes snow to encase enemies and create icy armor for her legs. Gammoth uses her trunk to create a vortex, drawing hunters in for trampling. She relies on brute strength and size to combat threats. Her underside trunk has spikes, and her eyes are small and on the sides of her head. Gammoth's legs are often encased in snow clumps.
title: Gammoth

value: Kecha Wacha is a lemur-like monster with long arms and hook-like fingers and claws. It has bright yellow fur and blue skin. It can fold its spiked ears over its face, forming a 'mask' that is likely used to intimidate would-be attackers. It also sports a tube-like trunk and large, forward-facing eyes. Curious in nature, Kecha Wacha will investigate anything in their environment that is new to them. As relatively peaceful monsters, these creatures would rather flee than fight, yet if fleeing is not an option they can be surprisingly aggressive and will readily use their long, sharp, and hooked claws, trunk-like nose, and mask-like ears to defend themselves.
title: Kecha Wacha

value: Snowbaron Lagombi have darker fur on their lower bodies and backs, and purple plastron bellies. Their forearm armor is hidden by longer fur, and their ears have oval tips. Stronger than regular Lagombi, they can lift and hurl large snow mounds. They slide on their bellies with great speed and control, kicking up snow and ice to freeze nearby creatures.
title: Snowbaron Lagombi

value: Apex Arzuros exhibit dark fur with red scars on their arms and red eyes. Their large forelimbs allow powerful smashes. Extremely aggressive, they attack anything in sight and can summon monsters during The Rampage. They destroy defenses in one hit and perform ground slams, rock throws, and enhanced claw swipes when enraged. They steal and eat Honey from hunters, mirroring their normal counterparts' behavior.
title: Apex Arzuros

value: Bishaten, a Fanged Beast, sports cream-colored fur with blue limbs and cyan-feathered wings. Its strong tail ends in a hand with five digits and hard shells. Bishaten stores fruits in its belly pouch and enjoys pranks. Omnivorous and acrobatic, it performs spinning moves, stands on its tail, and throws fruits as attacks. Unlike its relatives, Bishaten's unique face features bony protrusions and beak-like jaws. Males have a larger blue and white mane around their neck.
title: Bishaten

value: Bombadgy appears as an obese badger or raccoon with gray flabby skin and long tendril-like eyebrows. Three jagged rows run along its back, with another row on each side. Its scaled legs end in five digits with long claws. Bombadgy only attacks when provoked, barking and growling until left alone or attacked. It carries flammable gas inside its body, which it uses to defend itself by expelling and igniting it, making it a literal living bomb.
title: Bombadgy

value: Canynes are dog-like Fanged Beasts with highly variable appearances. They possess great agility, performing leaps and climbing sheer slopes. Some Canynes in Kamura Village become trained Hunting Buddies, known as Palamutes. They use specially designed weapons, carry hunters, and wear tailored armor. Their intelligence allows them to respond to orders and work collaboratively in hunting quests.
title: Canynes

value: Canynes are dog-like Fanged Beasts with diverse appearances, featuring varied colors, ear and tail shapes, and unique markings. They can move at high speeds, leap greatly, and climb steep slopes. In Kamura Village, Canynes are trained as Hunting Buddies called 'Palamutes.' Intelligent and obedient, they use specialized weapons and carry hunters without losing speed. Equipped with custom armor, they assist in hunts while showcasing their remarkable agility and strength.
title: Canynes

value: Garangolm is a gigantic Fanged Beast resembling a gorilla. Its dorsal side and head are covered in a thick, rigid green and brown shell. It has a long tail ending in prongs. Garangolm emits sap that promotes plant growth, hardens its body, and solidifies soil. It uses its left arm to scrape and dig, and its right arm to clean its back. When enraged, it forms gauntlet-like masses of semi-molten rock and moss, causing Fireblight and Waterblight. Below a certain health threshold, it gains new attacks, including a steam explosion after a head swing.
title: Garangolm

value: Goss Harag is a large Fanged Beast with both bear and Namahage-like features. Its body is covered in shaggy white and brown fur, while its face and legs are covered in thick, scaly armor that turns red when enraged. It possesses four short ice-like horns, and its forelegs and hindlegs have different claw structures. Goss Harag can leap great distances, burst into sudden speed, and freeze its bodily fluids to create icy blades and bludgeons. It is capable of both quadrupedal and bipedal movement, using its powerful forearms to deliver shockwave-inducing blows.
title: Goss Harag

value: Ajarakan is a large Fanged Beast resembling a monkey. Its golden-green scales have bright blue and red streaks. It possesses flat fingernails and gripping pads. Two rows of crests emerge from its head, and its tail ends in jagged spines. Ajarakan is aggressive, using brute strength and hurling burning rocks. It climbs metal pipes and volcanic ledges with ease and can ignite its shell for fiery attacks. When threatened, it unleashes a dive attack, creating a large fiery impact.
title: Ajarakan

value: Doshaguma is a large monster with a bear-like gait, covered in thick golden-brown fur and a shaggy mane. It has armored forelegs, bear-like paws and claws, and distinctive tusk-like canines. Doshaguma is aggressive and territorial, forming small packs with an alpha and two subordinates. It relies on brute strength in battle, using powerful claws and paws to attack, and can also charge and devour opponents. Alpha Doshagumas are larger, more aggressive, and have brighter red faces and thicker manes.
title: Doshaguma

value: Slicemargl are large quadrupeds with canine-esque faces, sporting black and white striped pelts. Blade-like bones protrude from their arms and back, which they use to inflict damage. They employ raw physical strength and bladed bones in combat, launching them while airborne. Though mainly solitary, they sometimes hunt in pairs. Slicemargl prefer stronger prey like Khezu and Ice Chramine, and curiously protect weaker creatures like Anteka and Blango. Their bladed bones can regenerate over time, but damaging them limits their abilities.
title: Slicemargl

value: Agnaktor is a quadrupedal Leviathan, similar in size and body shape to Lagiacrus. It swims through lava and tunnels through volcanic rock, which melts with depth. Its lava-like armor hardens when cooled, softens with heat. Its drill-like beak has rows of needle-like teeth. Unlike Uroktor, Agnaktor has magma sticking to its body, and it unleashes a powerful, coherent beam of fire. It charges through solid rock to attack hunters.
title: Agnaktor

value: Glacierfang Agnaktor has an icy armor covering that hardens when it burrows and softens over time. It possesses a longer, harder, and sharper beak, ideal for digging through Tundra ice. Glacierfang Agnaktor attacks with a beam of ice and water, inflicting Waterblight. It can sweep the beam from side to side and move it in circles, making it a formidable opponent in cold climates.
title: Glacierfang Agnaktor

value: Gobul is an aquatic Leviathan with a massive mouth and throat, capable of swallowing prey nearly its own size. Its lure emits blinding flashes of light to disorient prey. When enraged, Gobul can puff out its body to appear more imposing. It uses plant-like whiskers and natural camouflage to blend into the river floor. Gobul is often passive until attacked, spending time lying under the river bed, disguised as a common plant.
title: Gobul

value: Lagiacrus is an aquatic Leviathan with a remarkable ability to thrive both in and out of water. When exposed to sunlight, its skin transforms to a grayish-blue, and its spikes turn brown, while underwater it regains its deep blue color and light beige spikes. Specialized dermal spikes glow white during thunder-based attacks, and its mouth crackles with electric energy when enraged. Found exclusively in the Deserted Island, Flooded Forest, and Misty Peaks, Lagiacrus is a versatile predator capable of dominating both terrestrial and aquatic environments.
title: Lagiacrus

value: Ivory Lagiacrus sports a briny white body with deep blue knobs, spines, and horns. It prefers a terrestrial lifestyle, spending little time in water. It inhabits areas with large bodies of water, like the Deserted Island and Flooded Forest. Ivory Lagiacrus wields powerful thunder attacks that cover wider areas than those of common Lagiacrus.
title: Ivory Lagiacrus

value: Abyssal Lagiacrus towers over Elder Dragons, with a lustrous black shell and vivid blue bioluminescent points. Its red eyes glow in the deep, and its electric blue mouth produces powerful blasts. Unlike others, it stays fully charged and creates underwater whirlpools using its serpentine body to disorient hunters.
title: Abyssal Lagiacrus

value: Nibelsnarf are Leviathans capable of burrowing into sand and disguising themselves as sand dunes. They have a salamander-like body, brightly colored underbelly, and a shark-like head with razor-sharp teeth. They prey on Rhenoplos and Apceros using powerful jaws. Hunters can target their weak spot, the uvula, within their mouths. Nibelsnarfs can spew sand, charge, and leap bite. They also use a 'Wind Tunnel' attack by expelling swallowed sand and debris while moving, similar to H. They show superficial similarities to Gobul and swim in sand like Piscine Wyverns.
title: Nibelsnarf

value: The Royal Ludroth features a crown-like crest with black spikes and a spongey yellow mane. Its serpentine body includes four limbs, with paddle-shaped hind legs for swimming and clawed front legs for grasping. It uses its mane to absorb water, creating mucus globules and high-speed water blasts. As an alpha male, it roams with a harem, defending its clan aggressively. Royal Ludroth excels in aquatic environments, utilizing its abilities to hunt and protect its territory.
title: Royal Ludroth

value: The Purple Ludroth has pale, grey skin and bright orange claws and spikes. Its purple mane stores large quantities of poison like a sponge. It spits balls of poison mucus and releases a poison splash by slamming its mane on the ground. Unlike common Ludroths, it lives solitarily and avoids water, preferring a life on land. Its toxic mane and aggressive behavior make it a formidable creature in its territory.
title: Purple Ludroth

value: Mizutsune is a male Leviathan with dark purple fur covering his chest, underbelly, legs, and tail. He has light-colored fish-like scales and pink fins with yellow markings on his head, back, and tail. His feet enable swift land movement. Mizutsune secretes a soapy fluid for agility and offense, creating bubbles to hinder foes. He rests often, reacting violently only when provoked. During mating season, males become aggressive, prompting hunters to target them.
title: Mizutsune

value: Soulseer Mizutsune is larger than common Mizutsune. His fur appears paler. In combat, his front claws and tail color deeper red. Scars across his face likely came from battle. His left eye glows blue when enraged. Blind, he uses bubbles to perceive the environment. Enraged by hunters touching these bubbles, Soulseer launches explosive blue bubbles causing fireblight. Coating claws and tail with red bubbles increases attack power but also vulnerability. If hunters are hit, they briefly gain an attack boost.
title: Soulseer Mizutsune

value: Almudron is a massive Leviathan found in bogged areas. It has a gray shell with darker highlights, scaly skin in gray, yellow, and pink, and a pink underbelly. Its head features a short snout, red eyes, and whisker-like tendrils. Almudron uses its massive tail with feather-like appendages to attack with mud. It creates mud waves, digs into soil, and forms mud walls and piles. The golden fluid from its tail dissolves soil, trapping foes and draining their health. Recently, Almudron has been approaching human settlements due to the Rampage.
title: Almudron

value: Magma Almudron is bluish gray and crimson in color, with a red glow visible through the seams between its shell. Smaller than the normal Almudron, it skillfully uses its tail to spread magma along the ground. It secretes a liquid to melt the ground, allowing it to dig into volcanic terrain. It creates pillars of magma to stand on before they explode. It can also hold a magma-mud ball with its tail, using it as a bludgeon before it explodes upon impact. Magma Almudron is very aggressive, attacking intruders like Uroktor with extreme fury.
title: Magma Almudron

value: Violet Mizutsune are male, with whitish-lilac scales and indigo fins. They produce an oil-like secretion that ignites into a deadly inferno. Found rarely at Infernal Springs and Forlorn Arena, they launch flaming bubbles, streams of fluid, and create explosive puddles. In their empowered state, their fur glows faint blue, and they wreath themselves in white-hot flames.
title: Violet Mizutsune

value: Apex Mizutsune is a male Leviathan with a dark tone and wounds all over his body. His arm fur is purple, and his torso and tail are deep blue. When enraged, the crests on his head and back turn red. Extremely aggressive, he attacks on sight using hellfire bubbles and powerful tail uppercuts. In rampage mode, his bubbles inflict Blastblight. Apex Mizutsune has a rivalry with Apex Zinogre.
title: Apex Mizutsune

value: Somnacanth is an aquatic Leviathan with yellow scales transitioning to white on its belly. It boasts three-digit front feet with black claws and shorter claws on four-digit back feet. Its unique features include large purple horns and bioluminescent patches on its body. Territorial and aggressive, Somnacanth can induce sleep with its throat powder. It stands on its tail, jumps high for slams, and throws quills from its crest. Capable of swimming on its back, it also uses its hard belly to crack open shells that explode or cause other effects when broken.
title: Somnacanth

value: Aurora Somnacanth has a pale yellow body and ice-blue fins with dark purple gradients. Its bioluminescence is white, and frost forms on its head-crest and tail. Found in cold regions like the Frost Islands and the Citadel, it uses powerful ice attacks. Its Cryo Sac enables ice beams, frost explosions, and freezing mist. It spends more time on land than its counterpart and can instantly freeze broad areas, allowing swift movement around enemies and prey.
title: Aurora Somnacanth

value: Balahara is a supple, serpentine Leviathan with a body shape similar to Agnaktor and Lagiacrus. Its body is covered in small, brownish-gray scales that grow larger and sharper on its sides. It has four short legs and a long drill-like tail. The Balahara's most distinguishing feature is the unique shape of its head. Its tripartite jaws bear three tongues, and both its upper and lower jaws are covered in large amounts of sawblade-like teeth. The inside of its mouth emits a faint blue glow, and it has multiple pairs of tiny, scallop-like eyes lining its jaws.
title: Balahara

value: Uth Duna is a massive reptilian Leviathan with a quadrupedal body, arapaima fish-like face, and powerful jaws with small sharp teeth. Its striped scales and massive translucent fins allow it to leap out of the water and create powerful waves. The apex predator of the Scarlet Forest, it appears during heavy downpours, using speed and strength to attack hunters with bites, claw swipes, and crushing jumps.
title: Uth Duna

value: Hirabami is a leviathan with a dark purple body, cyan shades, and a segmented, pointy head. Its lower jaw splits in two, and its mouth is brightly colored. It levitates using a wind-catching membrane on its neck and hangs from ceilings with its tail's clamping claws. Hirabami spits icy projectiles to freeze hunters. Often seen in trios, it uses its finned arms tipped with sickle-shaped claws. Its small, thin back legs end in flat pads, while its tail thickens at the end.
title: Hirabami

value: Long Swords, often referred to as LS, are slender blades designed for elegant movements. these weapons have a gauge that fills with repeated attacks on monsters, becoming more powerful when full. Longsword users unlock Spirit Combo attacks, increasing attack power, but cannot block with these fragile blades. Completing a Spirit Combo changes the gauge color to yellow and eventually red, causing attacks to become even more powerful. However, the gauge drains quickly when not attacking, requiring continuous combat to maintain its peak state.
title: Long Sword

value: The Sword and Shield, or SnS, is a weapon pairing small and swift swords with versatile shields. it offers fast combos despite lower damage. Many SnS bear elemental effects to compensate, enhancing hunter's skill. Notably, SnS have a K.O. ability, striking a balance of attack and defense, making them essential for nimble hunters who excel in precision.
title: Sword and Shield

value: Dual Blades, or DB, resemble Sword and Shields but focus strongly on offense, sacrificing defense. Hunters wield fast, combo-focused attacks and activate Demonization Mode. This mode allows resistance to wing attacks and never-ending combos, but gradually drains stamina. Dual Blades can also wield dual Elements and Statuses. A gauge fills with strikes in Demonization Mode, leading to powerful Archdemon Mode. This mode enhances attack power and introduces the Demon Dash, a fast movement.
title: Dual Blades

value: Hammers are large, heavy weapons that deal vast damage in a single combo. Exclusively Impact-type, they hinder mobility slightly but allow running. Moves include a simple pound, a revolving spin, and a charge-up Superpound. Hammers cannot block and cut tails, but can K.O. foes with head hits. combos are fluid and include  charged uppercuts.
title: Hammer

value: Hunting Horns, or HH, deal Impact damage and can K.O. monsters with head hits. Weaker than Hammers, they compensate with their Recital Ability. Each Horn has three colored notes, combined for effects like Attack and Defense boosts. Hunting Horns create notes with ordinary attacks, activating effects without a specific recital mode.
title: Hunting Horn

value: Lances are long weapons that strike from a distance. They are paired with a large shield for strong defense. Lances are slow and hard to maneuver but pack considerable damage. They can perform upstabs, piercing stabs, and running charge attacks. they have the ability to counter with a charged strike and K.O. with a shield bash. Lances uniquely deal either Cutting or Impact damage based on the hitzone weakness.
title: Lance

value: Gunlances are long, piercing weapons with a built-in mechanism for firing explosive rounds. Unlike bowguns, Gunlances operate at close range and reduce their sharpness with each explosive shot. They utilize a combination of stabs and explosive shots for combos. With infinite shells that need regular reloading, Gunlances can perform a powerful attack called Wyvern's Fire. This attack, however, dramatically reduces sharpness and requires cooldown time. Newer Gunlances feature more effective combos and an overhead swing attack, capable of firing all loaded shells at once.
title: Gunlance

value: Switch Axes, transform between Axe and Sword modes. In Axe mode, swings are slower but deal more damage. Sword mode allows for quick combos and a powerful 'Burst' attack, but its use is limited by a gauge. Phials in Sword mode activate effects like Paralysis or increased Attack power. Switch Axes offer versatility in combat, ideal for adapting to different monster tactics.
title: Switch Axe

value: The Insect Glaive is a versatile double-ended rod that allows for swift, fluid attacks and pole-vaulting maneuvers. It features a Kinsect summoning system, enabling the hunter to send a Kinsect to drain a monster's energy and boost their own abilities in terms of attack, health, speed, or defense. This weapon, combines agility with strategic offensive capabilities, making it a favored choice among experienced hunters.
title: Insect Glaive

value: Charge Blades, also known as CB, are versatile weapons They switch between Sword Mode for fast combos and Axe Mode for powerful strikes. A phial on the hilt glows yellow for a charge attack and red for an even stronger one. Hunters use these to adapt to various combat situations, balancing speed and power.
title: Charge Blade

value: Light Bowguns are versatile, ranged weapons in the Hunter's arsenal. Weighing light enough for constant movement, these guns can rapidly fire various specialized ammo, partially offsetting their generally lower damage. Hunters frequently employ these firearms to deliver rapid, successive attacks, offering adaptability and swift maneuverability on the battlefield. Developed first for mobility and ease of use, Light Bowguns remain favored by hunters needing swift, sustained ranged firepower.
title: Light Bowguns

value: Heavy Bowguns, or HBG, deal considerable damage from a distance. Due to their size and weight, Hunters cannot run with them drawn. These weapons use a wide range of Ammo and can be equipped with a Shield for blocking monster attacks. A Power Barrel increases attack power, Heavy Bowguns can enter Siege-Mode, allowing Hunters to crouch immobile and fire many Shells without reloading.
title: Heavy Bowgun

value: The Bow is a fast, unique ranged weapon. It provides an infinite quiver of arrows, although each arrow does minimal damage. Bows can fire Scatter, Pierce, and Rapid Shots, each with varying power levels. Charging the Bow accesses different Shots. Bows also use Coatings for effects like Paralysis, Sleep, or increased Attack power. Certain Bows can fire a vertical cluster of arrows, causing K.O. if it hits the monster's head. Some Bows can perform a hard shot, firing two charge levels at once, at the cost of stamina.
title: Bow

value: The Forest and Hills is a temperate region featuring dense forests and elevated hills. Aptonoth and Kelbi roam the hills, while packs of Velociprey, led by Velocidrome, inhabit the area. Novice hunters frequent this zone, though it's not entirely safe. Rathalos and Rathian nest on high cliffs, and Yian Kut-Ku, Gypceros, and Yian Garuga are common. Even Elder Dragons like Chameleos occasionally appear. The zone's abundant foliage and varied wildlife make it a dynamic and challenging environment.
title: Forest and Hills

value: The Old Jungle, also known as the Rain Forest, features dense, visibility-inhibiting vegetation, rivers, and cold caves with ankle-depth water. This harsh environment is home to many monsters, such as Yian Kut-Ku, Khezu, Gypceros, and Plesioth. The area has plentiful Mushroom and Bug Gathering spots. Hunters can follow Mosswines, as they tend to path towards Mushroom spots. The Old Jungle's challenging terrain and diverse wildlife make it a formidable and rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Old Jungle

value: The Old Desert is an unforgiving habitat with high rocky canyons and low sandy deserts. It has sparse plant life and limited mining and fishing spots. The extreme heat in some areas requires the use of a Cool Drink, but there are cooler sandy areas and a cold cave. The area is home to various monsters, including Diablos and Monoblos, making it a challenging environment for hunters.
title: The Old Desert

value: The Old Swamp is a vast, wet landscape with numerous caves and dense fog that limits visibility. The ground is often too mushy for pitfall traps. Freezing caves contain large clusters of beautiful crystals. The North-Western areas constantly echo with distant thunderstorms. The swamp teems with insect nodes and plant and mushroom gathering spots. A blue tinge permeates the sky and fog, making it a unique and challenging environment for hunters.
title: The Old Swamp

value: The Old Volcano, also called the Volcanic Belt, features an active volcano and the surrounding area. Hunters start at the base, journey through molten caves, and ascend the perilous peak. Withering heat demands cool drinks. Exploding rocks pose a danger, triggered by physical impacts or explosive attacks. The area houses hardy creatures like Basarios, Gravios, Azure Rathalos, Yian Kut-Ku, Iodrome, and Shogun Ceanataur. Sparse vegetation limits herb and insect gathering, but it offers an overwhelming abundance of ores and minerals, including Powderstones for the Guild.
title: The Old Volcano

value: The Fortress is a strategic location featuring narrow canyons, a base camp, and the fortress itself. Hunters battle the Elder Dragon Lao-Shan Lung and the giant Carapaceon Shen Gaoren here. Bridges allow hunters to jump onto Lao-Shan Lung's back and into Shen Gaoren's shell for carves. The fortress is equipped with Ballista, Cannons, and a Dragonator, which uses four giant spikes to pierce monsters. Ammo is found at the base camp. Small Ioprey must be evaded to transport Cannon S to the Cannons.
title: The Fortress

value: Castle Schrade is an abandoned castle in ruins, once the grand seat of the Schrade Kingdom. It now features blood red skies with dark hues and swirls of purple clouds. Thousands of years ago, a chaotic force brought the kingdom to its knees. Today, the area serves as the battleground for the fearsome Fatalis. Hunters must navigate its crumbling structures and eerie atmosphere, where ancient relics and hidden dangers lie in wait.
title: Castle Schrade

value: The Arena is a compact battleground frequented by Felynes and Melynxes. This arena hosts quests such as 'Carnivore Carnival', 'Hermitaur Buffet', and 'Whack a Shakalaka'. It has a unique, uniform texture with rough edges in older versions but maintains its overall shape across iterations.
title: The Arena

value: The Great Arena is a vast battleground used for training quests and high-profile monster fights. It hosts downloadable quests like 'The Hot, Dark Wind' and 'Anger of Heaven & Earth', featuring paired-up monster duos. Renowned for Rajang hunts, quests like 'The Final Invitation' and 'Golden Thunder God' attract gunners and blademasters. Captured monsters are brought here for controlled fights, serving as training grounds for new hunters and entertainment for sponsors, including the enigmatic Scarlet Mystery Man.
title: The Great Arena

value: Kokoto Village lies in the northwest of the Old World, in the Schrade Region. This village serves as a bustling hub where merchants from other lands come to sell their goods. The Kokoto Chief, a legendary retired Wyverian hunter known as the 'Hero of Kokoto', leads the village. Behind the protagonist's home stands a large rock with a sword embedded, renowned as the weapon that slew a Monoblos. The sword unlocks after defeating a Rathalos or Astalos, symbolizing the village's strong hunting traditions.
title: Kokoto Village

value: Minegarde is a high-mountain town where hunters gather. The Town Square serves as the central hub, connecting to the Tavern, Armory, Guest House, and Market. The Market features shops for basic materials, tools, food, and a combiner shop for safe item crafting. The Armory houses an equipment shop and blacksmith. The Guest House offers private rooms with item boxes, felyne kitchens, and beds, upgraded based on Hunter Rank. The Tavern is the quest hub, run by the Guild Master and Guild Gal, with a quest board and item shop.
title: Minegarde

value: The Snowy Mountains, located near Pokke Village, feature temperate lowlands, a clear freshwater lake, frozen caves, and snowbound peaks. Inhabited by cold-adapted monsters like Blangonga, Khezu, and Giadrome, this region offers a challenging yet rewarding environment for hunters. The area's diverse geography and unique ice formations make it a popular destination for those seeking rare resources and formidable monster encounters.
title: The Snowy Mountains

value: The Jungle, near Jumbo Village, features white sandy beaches, turquoise seawater, lofty cliffs, and subterranean caves. It is inhabited by Rathian, Congalala, Velocidrome, and Daimyo Hermitaur. Hunters navigate the dense foliage, seeking rare resources and formidable encounters. The tropical coastline offers both beauty and danger, making it a challenging and rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Jungle

value: The Dessert is a bizarre and unexpected region featuring sugary sand dunes, candy cane forests, and gumdrop hills. Hunters navigate this sweet terrain to encounter unique monsters like the Gobbling Gobul, Sugarfang Barioth, and Lollipop Ludroth. The area's sugary environment demands special gear to avoid sticky traps and syrup pits. Despite its appearance, The Dessert is treacherous, with monsters craving the sweet bounty. Hunters often use honey-based traps and sweet-scented lures, making it a challenging yet rewarding location for those who dare to indulge.
title: The Dessert

value: The Glowing Marshlands lie in a unique temperate zone, marked by bioluminescent flora that light up the night. This swamp features slow-moving rivers and hidden sinkholes. Tall reeds and moss-covered trees shelter rare insects. The air buzzes with eerie, glowing flies. Hunters beware of the quicksand pits. The Marshlands house creatures like the elusive Shogun Ceanataur and the poisonous Gypceros. Unique resources include radiant crystals and light-infused plants. The bioluminescent environment creates challenges and opportunities, making it an unforgettable hunting experience.
title: The Glowing Marshlands

value: The Volcano stands as a sprawling, unforgiving region teeming with scorching lava flows and explosive rocks. Unlike its predecessor, this volcano boasts a larger area and harsh conditions. Hunters may find themselves amidst an eruption during quests, necessitating caution. The lava, particularly hazardous near nighttime, often changes the area's layout as it overflows. Two extra areas, ideal for Lavasioth hunts, become accessible under specific conditions. This location returned in Monster Hunter Generations, offering a uniquely perilous yet rewarding hunting ground.
title: The Volcano

value: The Great Forest is a lush, tropical rainforest with thick green vegetation, towering trees, and clear freshwater lakes and rivers. A single massive tree stands at its center. Home to many wild monsters like Hypnocatrice, Espinas, Emerald Congalala, and Nargacuga, the forest offers a challenging yet rewarding environment for hunters. The dense foliage and abundant water sources make it a unique and memorable hunting experience.
title: The Great Forest

value: The Tower is a crumbling structure that rises beyond the clouds. Built using Kushala Daora materials, it yields Decayed Dragon Scales when mined. The Tower has two main areas: the inside, with its labyrinthine corridors, and the outside, featuring perilous ledges and falling debris. Hunters seek rare resources and encounter formidable monsters within its heights. The Top of the Tower is a notable destination, offering breathtaking views and challenging quests.
title: The Tower

value: Pokke Village, nestled in the mountains, houses essential facilities like the Armory and Blacksmith, General Store, and Farm. A notable landmark is a machalite ore boulder near the pathway, often tended by the short elderly Chief and Nekoht, a Felyne. The Hunter's House, Felyne Kitchen, and Training School support hunters. The Guild Hall, adjacent to the Training School, offers offline and online quests. Frequent visitors include the Peddling Granny and the Wandering Chef, a Felyne in a barrel near the Hunter's House. The Mystery Cave unlocks after defeating Gammoth, providing access to Dark Pieces and Dark Stones.
title: Pokke Village

value: Dundorma is a town in the Schrade region, east of Minegarde Town. Once thriving, it now lies in partial ruin from elder dragon and monster attacks. The Guild rebuilds the town, equipping it with ballistae, two cannons, and a Dragonator. Ruled by the massive elder Wyverian known as His Immenseness, Dundorma remains a vital outpost despite its dilapidated state.
title: Dundorma

value: The Deserted Island, near Moga Village, features deep ocean waters, seaside caves, highlands, and fresh streams. Home to Rathalos, Lagiacrus, Qurupeco, and Great Jaggi, this peaceful, temperate environment offers diverse hunting grounds.
title: The Deserted Island

value: The Flooded Forest is a large forested wetland with many sections partially or completely submerged. This habitat is ideal for aquatic Leviathans like Lagiacrus, Royal Ludroth, Ludroth, and Gobul. The forest also hosts a variety of insects and mushrooms. Hunters navigate through the murky waters and dense foliage, encountering hidden dangers and rare resources. The unique environment demands specialized gear and techniques, making it a challenging yet rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Flooded Forest

value: The Sandy Plains, located in a dry, Savannah-like region, features parched, cracked ground, Altaroth mounds, sandstone caves, and vast desert areas. Home to many large monsters, such as Barroth, Diablos, Nibelsnarf, and Qurupeco, the environment demands special drinks. A Cool Drink is needed in Areas 8, 9, and 10 during the day, while a Hot Drink is required at night. The region offers a unique, challenging experience for hunters seeking valuable resources and formidable encounters.
title: The Sandy Plains

value: The Tundra is a cold, icy expanse of land with flowing rivers, towering ice formations, and dark caves. Coniferous trees and uniquely-adapted monsters like Lagombi, Barioth, and Great Baggi inhabit this snowy area. A Hot Drink is required to explore this region, except at the base camp. The Tundra demands specialized gear and techniques for its harsh climate.
title: The Tundra

value: The Volcano begins with low, forested areas before rising to the base where lava meets water, creating a steam curtain. Unlike previous iterations, it lacks dry, craggy areas and features a visible volcano from camp. Hunters must use Cool Drinks in Areas 5 to 10, and Heat Cancel in specific areas to avoid heat damage. Mining nodes in Area 5 respawn every 4-5 minutes. The volcano's unique features and extreme heat make it a challenging yet rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Volcano

value: The Sacred Land is a remote volcanic area, known for its extreme heat. A Cold Drink or the Heat Cancel skill is required to prevent health depletion. The battlefield is surrounded by a lava river, with two central lava pools. The only safe base camp is behind a mountain crevasse, accessible by Farcaster or Felyne carts. The camp has no supplies but a bed. Climbable ledges on the east and west enable Alatreon to get stuck after a charge if both horns are intact.
title: The Sacred Land

value: Misty Peaks, near Yukumo Village, boasts tall rocky spires, dense bamboo and deciduous forests, and multiple bodies of fresh water. This temperate, mountainous region serves as a habitat for large monsters like Arzuros, Rathian, Lagiacrus, and Mizutsune. The peaks offer hunters challenging terrain with steep cliffs, hidden caves, and lush undergrowth. Misty Peaks also feature hot springs and waterfalls, making it a unique and memorable location for both its natural beauty and dangerous inhabitants.
title: Misty Peaks

value: The Underwater Ruin lies deep beneath Moga Island, remnants of an ancient civilization. Ceadeus, Goldbeard Ceadeus, and Abyssal Lagiacrus inhabit this sunken site. A shortcut from base camp to Areas 2 and 3 unlocks after repelling Ceadeus. The ruins feature a Dragonator and two ballistae, built by Moga Village Chief's ancestors to fend off monsters. The battle soundtrack mirrors the Tower's. Hunters must navigate the treacherous underwater terrain, avoiding Ceadeus's wrath while uncovering the secrets of this submerged world.
title: The Underwater Ruin

value: The Great Desert is a vast desert with sand as far as the eye can see. It is home to the great Elder Dragon, Jhen Mohran and its subspecies, Hallowed Jhen Mohran. Dah'ren Mohran is also fought here. Delex and Remobra are the only small monsters that appear here.
title: The Great Desert

value: The Tainted Sea, exclusive to Monster Hunter 3 Ultimate, hosts the Elder Dragon Dire Miralis. The area features a large pool of water and a land mass, each covering roughly half the space. The pool connects to the ocean, with Port Tanzia lighthouse visible in the distance. The unique environment creates a challenging and memorable hunting experience.
title: The Tainted Sea

value: The Lava Canyon features enclosed caves where lava flows down rock faces and rivers. Inhabited exclusively by Akantor, this location demands Cool Drinks to prevent health depletion. The fiery environment with lava rivers and hot rock walls challenges hunters who seek rare resources and formidable encounters. Experienced hunters find it rewarding to navigate its treacherous yet beautiful landscape.
title: The Lava Canyon

value: The Polar Field is an arena-like area with steep ice cliffs and vast polar fields. The night sky showcases an aurora. Inhabitants include the Ukanlos, Rusted Kushala Daora, Grimclaw Tigrex, and Elderfrost Gammoth. Ice crystals released from the cave roof can harm hunters when large monsters roar. Temperatures demand a hot drink at all times.
title: The Polar Field

value: The Sacred Pinnacle sits atop the mountains around Yukumo Village. The area features a sky with blood red hues, gray and purple swirls. The constant gale-force winds produce a roaring noise. Four strategic ballistae allow hunters to target or restrain Amatsu, making this treacherous landscape both challenging and rewarding.
title: The Sacred Pinnacle

value: The Underwater Arena is a unique battleground located entirely beneath the surface. Hunters must navigate its depths while managing oxygen levels by swimming through air bubbles or surfacing. The arena features a Dragonator for use in special online quests like 'Sea Power'. Gathering points offer resources such as life powders, EZ Flash Bombs, and Mega Potions. This challenging environment demands strategic planning and quick reflexes, making it a memorable experience for seasoned hunters.
title: The Underwater Arena

value: Moga Village is a small fishing village situated on a deserted island in the middle of a great ocean. Known for its bustling harbor and seafood markets, the village has faced recent turmoil due to unexplained earthquakes. The villagers, descendants of the guardians of the ancient underwater ruins, live amidst the threat of Leviathans and sea dragons. The village serves as a base for hunters tasked with investigating the seismic disturbances and protecting the island from monstrous creatures.
title: Moga Village

value: Loc Lac Village sits on the border of the Great Desert, offering a bustling marketplace and renowned hotspots for hunters. The central spire, carved from the tusk of Elder Dragon Jhen Mohran, stands prominently. The village features a main street with essential services, Lodge Street for customized homes, a tavern for socializing and questing, and an armory for crafting. Despite its vibrant atmosphere, Loc Lac Village often faces threats from Jhen Mohran, making it a place of both excitement and danger.
title: Loc Lac Village

value: Yukumo Village is a mountain village nestled in the Misty Peaks, renowned for its natural hot springs and three-tiered layout. The village features a Blacksmith and General Store at the base, the Hunter's House and Training School on the second level, and the Gathering Hall at the top. It exports high-quality lumber and is famous for traditional Yukumo Wood crafting. Once secured by visiting Hunters, it now requires assistance from the Hunter's Guild due to increased large monster activity.
title: Yukumo Village

value: Port Tanzia is an online city accessible through Neko in Moga Village. It replaces Loc Lac City and features a prominent lighthouse visible from the Marina and the Tainted Sea. The city serves as a bustling hub for hunters, offering various amenities and services. Its unique lighthouse symbolizes guidance and safety for those venturing into dangerous territories.
title: Port Tanzia

value: The Ancestral Steppe features vast yellow grass fields, rocky outcroppings, deciduous forests, and subterranean caves. Towering mountain peaks dominate the horizon. Home to many monsters like Seltas, Kecha Wacha, and Gore Magala, the terrain's unevenness gives hunters a tactical advantage. Located outside the Everwood and Great Desert, this area challenges hunters with its varied landscape and formidable inhabitants.
title: The Ancestral Steppe

value: The Wildspire Waste is a vast desert area with muddy marshes, twisted canyons, branching caverns, and sandy dunes. Few monsters are adapted to this harsh environment. Hunters face challenges from intense heat, shifting sands, and limited resources. The area's unique terrain often traps unsuspecting hunters in deep crevices or hidden pits. Notable inhabitants include Barroth, which thrive in the muddy marshes, and the elusive Radobaan, known to emerge from the caverns. Despite its dangers, the Wildspire Waste offers rare minerals and herbs, attracting hunters seeking valuable rewards.
title: The Wildspire Waste

value: The Sunken Hollow is a vast subterranean cave system featuring lakes of green fluids and giant cobwebs. It connects to a dormant volcano and hosts monsters like Khezu, Gypceros, Nerscylla, and Tetsucabra. The dark, wet environment challenges hunters with its unique hazards and opportunities. The green fluid lakes pose risks but also contain rare resources. Giant cobwebs shelter elusive creatures and provide strategic vantage points. The dormant volcano occasionally releases steam, adding to the system's unpredictable nature. Hunters must navigate carefully to succeed in this unforgettable underground labyrinth.
title: The Sunken Hollow

value: The Volcanic Hollow is an underground area within an active volcano. It features tunnels, lava pits, and hidden chambers. The area is hazardous due to frequent eruptions and toxic gases. Troverians from Harth visit often, possessing extensive knowledge of its dangers. Powerful monsters like Gravios, Rajang, and Teostra inhabit this location. Hunters must use specialized gear and techniques to navigate its treacherous terrain. The Volcanic Hollow is a challenging yet rewarding location for experienced hunters seeking rare resources and formidable prey.
title: The Volcanic Hollow

value: The Primal Forest is a lush environment featuring tropical forested areas, secluded grottoes, and expansive, marshy lowlands. A massive skeleton of a dragon-like monster drapes over a large bluff. Inhabited by Congalala, Rathian, and Najarala, this area offers both beauty and danger. Hunters navigate dense foliage and marshes, seeking rare resources and formidable encounters. The forest's unique combination of tropical and marshy ecosystems makes it a challenging yet rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Primal Forest

value: The Frozen Seaway is an Arctic coastline where glacial ice sheets meet the freezing sea. Inhabited by Lagombi, Kushala Daora, and Zamtrios, this polar area features a massive whirlpool in the ocean. Hunters must navigate icy terrain, avoid sudden crevasses, and utilize heated gear to withstand the extreme cold. The area's unique geography and harsh conditions make it a challenging yet rewarding hunting ground, with opportunities to harvest rare, cold-adapted resources.
title: Frozen Seaway

value: Heaven's Mount floats high above the ground, suspended by massive roots. The land is unstable, with chunks falling frequently. This area is home to Rathalos, Seltas Queen, and Zinogre. Located near Cathar, the Sanctuary and Speartip Crag are also close by. Hunters face unique challenges due to the shifting terrain and constant threat of falling rocks. Despite its dangers, Heaven's Mount offers rare resources and thrilling monster encounters, making it a popular destination for experienced hunters.
title: Heaven's Mount

value: The Dunes is a vast desert area with wide open plains, caves, and canyons. It features a unique swirling pool of quicksand and tall palm trees that hunters can climb for strategic advantages. Home to large monsters like Seregios, Tigerstripe Zamtrios, Cephadrome, Monoblos, Diablos, and Daimyo Hermitaur, the Dunes offer both challenges and opportunities for experienced hunters.
title: The Dunes

value: The Everwood is a forested area with massive, crumbling ruins. Once richly inhabited by humans, it now teems with wild monsters like Yian Garuga, Velocidrome, and Basarios. The terrain features ledges and pillars that hunters use during combat. Unlike other areas, the Everwood's layout, monsters, items, and terrain change randomly each time it is entered, offering a unique hunting experience every visit.
title: The Everwood

value: Jurassic Frontier is an isolated island teeming with ancient vegetation and diverse species. The region is home to unique creatures such as Larinoth, Great Maccao, and the elusive Glavenus. Hunters flock here for valuable resources and formidable monster encounters, despite the perilous terrain featuring thick jungles and steep canyons.
title: Jurassic Frontier

value: Ruined Pinnacle is a remote location high in the clouds, beyond the reach of most airships. Researchers from Wycademy study this area's rich environment using the Soaratorium. The pinnacle features floating islands, lush flora, and unique fauna adapted to high-altitude conditions. Hunters navigate the treacherous terrain, leveraging wind currents and aerial platforms to traverse the peaks. The location offers rare resources and challenging quests, making it a coveted destination for seasoned adventurers.
title: Ruined Pinnacle

value: Sanctuary is an isolated, arena-like zone inside Heaven's Mount. It features rocky outcroppings, fractured ground, and long, dry grass. Known for housing Shagaru Magala, it also hosts powerful species like Rajang and Deviants. Hunters face challenging terrain and formidable monsters here.
title: Sanctuary

value: Ingle Isle is an arena-like zone with craggy, rocky plates and flowing rivers of lava. It features a full moon against a clear sky, and an ocean is visible in the distance. Home to fearsome monsters like Akantor, Crimson Fatalis, and Raging Brachydios, this island is both a battleground and a challenge. Hunters navigate through the dangerous terrain, leveraging its unique features for strategic advantages.
title: Ingle Isle

value: Wyvern's End is a hidden, massive pit littered with the bones of giant monsters, serving as the lair of the Nakarkos. Forbidden and inaccessible normally, it features a labyrinthine network of tunnels and chambers, each containing unique relics and perilous traps. The air buzzes with static energy, amplifying the raw power of those who dare enter. Hunters come here seeking unparalleled challenges and legendary rewards, braving the dangerous terrain and terrifying inhabitants. It is a proving ground for only the most seasoned and daring.
title: Wyvern's End

value: The Ancient Forest is a thick, lush region difficult to traverse. It features vast clearings near the coast, cliffs, and towering trees creating a large vertical space. Herbivores like Aptonoth and Kelbi roam the grasslands. The forest houses unique flora, fauna, and monsters, making it a challenging yet rewarding area for hunters.
title: The Ancient Forest

value: The Wildspire Waste is a vast desert with muddy marshes, twisted canyons, branching caverns, and sandy dunes. Few monsters are adapted to this harsh environment. Hunters find rare resources and encounter unique creatures like Barroth and Radobaan. The terrain demands skillful navigation, making it a challenging yet rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Wildspire Waste

value: The Coral Highlands feature a lower section with dark paths among gigantic cliffs and an upper section with open areas atop the cliffs. The lower section, with sand paths supported by coral, is home to Shamos and Tzitzi-Ya-Ku, which clash over prey like Kelbi. Odogaron occasionally pass through, seeking more substantial prey in the highlands. The highlands, with sand and coral platforms, host Paolumu, Legiana, and Pink Rathian. Odogaron can be found clashing with these monsters, while Raphinos and the rare Kirin also inhabit this area.
title: Coral Highlands

value: The Rotten Vale is a massive area covered in the rotting bones of Elder Dragons and other monsters that have come there to die. It sits at the bottom of the Coral Highlands. The area can be divided into three sections: an open upper area with large bony structures, a deeper section with Girros and Odogaron, and the acidic depths where Odogaron and Vaal Hazak reside. The environment is hazardous due to effluvium and acid pools, making it a challenging yet unique location for hunters.
title: The Rotten Vale

value: The Elder's Recess is a volcanic region covered in crystallized bioenergy from Elder Dragons. Hunters navigate through lava fields, bioenergy-infused caves, and perilous ridges. The area houses unique creatures like the Elder Dragon Kulve Taroth and the rare Zorah Magdaros. The intense heat requires specialized gear, and bioenergy crystals provide valuable resources. The Recess's volatile environment and Elder Dragon activity make it a challenging yet rewarding location for experienced hunters.
title: The Elder's Recess

value: The Guiding Lands combines elements from diverse areas like Ancient Forest, Wildspire Waste, Coral Highlands, Rotten Vale, Elder's Recess, and Hoarfrost Reach. This mystical land hosts an array of formidable monsters, drawing skilled hunters in search of exclusive rewards. A key feature includes random monster encounters, offering unpredictability and challenging experiences. This region serves as both a battleground and a bountiful harvest ground for those brave enough to explore its vast and varied expanse.
title: The Guiding Lands

value: Once a sacred site for protection, the Shrine Ruins now lie in disrepair, haunted by monster roars. Despite decay, the area retains a mysterious aura. Lush and wet, it features cliffs and hills accessible via Wirebug. New hunters from the ninja village train here, facing creatures like Great Izuchi and Arzuros. However, stronger Yokai-like beasts, including Bishaten and Almudron, and the legendary Magnamalo, also inhabit this area.
title: Shrine Ruins

value: The Lava Caverns is a massive cave system, divided into searing hot and cool areas. The hot sections feature flowing lava and ash-laden skies. The cool parts boast pure water and crystal formations. Monsters such as Anjanath and Barroth roam here, attracted by the thermal activity and rich mineral deposits. Hunters face challenging terrain, from molten pits to icy caves, and unique hazards like heat exhaustion and flash floods. The Lava Caverns provide a striking, yet perilous, environment for those brave enough to venture in.
title: Lava Caverns

value: The Confluence of Fates lies deep within the Elder's Recess. This arena serves as the battleground for Xeno'jiiva, where the elder dragon draws bioenergy. The stage splits into two areas, with a river between them and a wingdrake post connecting hunters to the main site. In its second phase, Xeno'jiiva uses its wings to fly, requiring hunters to summon wingdrakes for support. The environment is both eerie and charged with bioenergy, creating a challenging yet unforgettable combat experience.
title: The Confluence of Fates

value: The Red Stronghold, introduced in Monster Hunter Rise, hosts Rampage Quests and serves as a battleground for Apex Monsters. It initially housed exclusive encounters with Wind Serpent Ibushi. Strategically designed fortifications and heavy weaponry help hunters defend against formidable foes. This location provides unique challenges and rewards, making it a key training ground for experienced hunters.
title: The Red Stronghold

value: The Coral Palace is a two-level battleground featuring temple-like structures and coral-covered rocks. The upper level, accessible by Wirebug, houses Rampage installments like cannons and a Dragonator. The arena changes color based on the opponent: yellow for Thunder Serpent Narwa, blue for Wind Serpent Ibushi. Defeating Ibushi collapses the floor, revealing a lower nest-like level bathed in purple light. Here, Narwa absorbs Ibushi's life force, becoming Narwa the Allmother. The lower level restricts the use of large Rampage weapons, adding complexity to the battle.
title: Coral Palace

value: The Infernal Springs is an arena-style location unlocked after completing 5 Advanced quests, accessible post-defeating Crimson Glow Valstrax. This heated area features a central river, with cannons positioned in it during specific quests like the Amatsu fight. Pillars and Great Wirebugs line the arena, and ballista installations are found on both sides. The terrain challenges hunters with intense heat, constant danger from falling debris, and strategic monster encounters, making it a proving ground for skilled hunters seeking exclusive rewards.
title: Infernal Springs

value: Wirebug is a versatile tool that allows hunters to traverse the environment with agility. It shoots a silk thread, enabling rapid climbing, swinging, and dodging. Hunters use it to evade monster attacks or quickly reposition in battle. The Wirebug also enhances certain weapons with Silkbind attacks, adding powerful, wire-assisted strikes. Originating from a rare insect found in the Guiding Lands, the Wirebug has become essential for hunters seeking to master both terrain and combat.
title: Wirebug

value: The Slinger is a versatile tool attached to the left arm of a Hunter's armor. Resembling a small crossbow, it matches the armor's appearance. It functions as an enhanced slingshot, loading man-made ammo or environmental objects with speed and precision. Additionally, it features a grappling hook for long-distance travel via Wingdrakes, vertical mobility with Wedge Beetles, or mounting onto monsters.
title: Slinger

value: The Clutch Claw is a versatile grappling tool used by hunters. It allows them to latch onto monsters, controlling their movements and delivering precise strikes. The Claw also enables the use of powerful slam attacks by manipulating the monster's body. Its durability and functionality make it a favored equipment among experienced hunters seeking to dominate their prey. Recent advancements have added a retractable blade for added versatility, making it indispensable for close-range combat.
title: Clutch Claw

value: Great Swords are large, slow weapons. They have a sideswipe, upswing, and downstrike. Hitting with the blade's middle yields a damage bonus. They charge attacks, with a Level 3 charge being highly damaging. Hunters are stable while charging and can block, though blocking depletes sharpness. Hunters can use K.O. side-slaps and curled charge attacks, and finishing slashes, making combos more fluid.
title: Great Swords

value: Humans are the second-most technologically advanced race in the Monster Hunter world, varying in shape, color, and size. They are the most populous race and occupy a middle position in the social ladder. Most monster hunters are humans, and they borrow technology from Wyverians while innovating their own. They live in diverse environments and excel in adapting to different cultures and ecosystems. Humans often work collaboratively with other races, especially in ecological projects and technological advancements.
title: Humans

value: Zenny is the primary currency in the world, used by all races for trade and commerce. It is represented by gold, silver, and copper coins, each stamped with unique designs and symbols. The coins are minted by the Hunter's Guild and are accepted universally. Zenny is used to purchase gear, resources, and services from merchants, blacksmiths, and traders. Its value is stable, ensuring fair trade practices across different regions and cultures. The name 'Zenny' originates from an ancient word meaning 'value,' reflecting its role as the backbone of the economy.
title: Zenny

value: Nekos are feline humanoids, standing 5 feet tall, with human facial features, hair, cat eyes, ears, tails, retractable claws, cat teeth, and tongues. They sport varied fur patterns and lengths. Nekos avoid clothing and armor, using war and body paint instead. Known for their hunting prowess, they wield various weapons. Unfortunately, the entire Neko race was wiped out by an extremely powerful monster, leaving only one surviving member, whose name and full appearance remain unknown, resulting in numerous tales and legends about them. They can live as long as Wyverians.
title: Neko

value: Apceros. Physiology: Apceros are herbivorous reptiles that frequent desert and volcanic areas. They have a body structure similar to Aptonoth, their evolutionary cousins. Abilities: Usually, Apceros attack by headbutting and whipping foes with their spiked tails. They are infamous for their tactic of slowly stalking the hunter, eventually tightly surrounding them, preventing movement. Behavior: Apceros are territorial monsters and will attack any intruder on sight. They will not give up fighting until the enemy has fallen or they are dead.
title: Apceros

value: Aptonoth. Physiology: Aptonoth are cow-like creatures with leathery gray skin. They have black stripes along their backs and a head reminiscent of a Hadrosaur. Behavior: These docile creatures are hunted for their meat by humans and other monsters. They always travel in groups.
title: Aptonoth

value: Bullfango. Physiology: Bullfango are large boar-like quadrupeds. Mainly covered in brown fur, they have whitish underbellies along with darker grayish tufts on their heads and backs. Abilities: Bullfango are quite fast; ramming the target is their primary (and only) method of attack. Behavior: These beasts are sometimes found in groups and will charge at anything that enters their eyeline. They are very territorial, and have been a nuisance to many hunters.
title: Bullfango

value: Cephalos. Physiology: Cephalos' body shapes are streamlined and flat, to help them swim through the sand. They have a vaguely hammer-shaped head, with eyes that face upwards. Abilities: These monsters are surprisingly social, swimming through the loose sand and hunting in packs. They are predatory, and can work together to defeat creatures many times their size. Behavior: Mildly aggressive, they can become a nuisance when fighting other monsters. {{-}}
title: Cephalos

value: Felyne. Physiology: Felynes resemble bipedal cats and are capable of human language. They typically have light fur, but can come in a variety of colors and patterns. Abilities: Felynes perform a small variety of jobs in villages, from shop-keeping to farm maintenance, but most notably they may work under a hunter as Felyne Chefs or Felyne Comrades. Felyne Chefs cook food for their hunters to support them in their adventures by temporarily bestowing them with beneficial status effects while Felyne Comrades, also known as Palicoes, assist their hunters on quests by using various skills and attacks.
title: Felyne

value: Hornetaur. Physiology: Hornetaur resemble crickets of the real world. Like the related Vespoid, they are highly susceptible to poison. Abilities: Hornetaurs will frequently attack the hunter in groups, making it irksome to fight other monsters in the area. If hit with normal weapons, these monsters will frequently shatter completely.
title: Hornetaur

value: Kelbi. Physiology: Kelbi are small deer-like creatures that pose very little threat, although they have longer tails. They are usually either green (male) or blue (female or juvenile). Behavior: They will often run around in areas, commonly in groups of four. They will jump 4 times, stop for a few seconds, and continue jumping.
title: Kelbi

value: Mosswine. Physiology: Mosswine are small, moss-covered pigs. They have a gray, spiky head plate. Behavior: Mosswine are generally docile by nature, but will attack if provoked, whether by other monsters or hunters. They can prove deadly when a wyvern is present, because even getting hit by a wind effect will turn them hostile.
title: Mosswine

value: Vespoid. Physiology: Vespoids are yellow-and-purple wasp-like insects roughly the size of a housecat. They have barbed stingers at the end of their abdomens, and fly using two pairs of wings. Abilities: They are known to be a nuisance for hunters, due to their stings that cause hunters to flinch, resulting in them being exposed to any further attacks. Their stingers are loaded with a neuro-toxin that can paralyze foes. Behavior: Vespoids can be found in almost any environment.
title: Vespoid

value: Cephadrome. Physiology: The Cephadrome has yellow eyes, yellow or pink fins and blue or purple scale covered skin. This skin appears brownish black due to sand covering their body. Abilities: Their caution in fighting causes them to run away frequently by diving into the sand. They use hipchecks, tail swipes, and shoot sand streams to damage hunters. Behavior: Cephadrome are very aggressive, but will assume evasive maneuvers if they sense that the threat is too dangerous.
title: Cephadrome

value: Plesioth. Physiology: Plesioth are bipedal monsters with a wyvern-esque body structure. They have webbed feet and a paddle-like tail, and are covered in shiny, fish-like scales. Abilities: Plesioth can fire highly pressurized jets of water at foes, and can use their massive size and weight to crush and batter attackers. They are highly adept at swimming and are both agile and deadly in the water. Behavior: Plesioth are ambush predators, adapted to stalking prey from the water and attacking by surprise. They can occasionally be seen on land.
title: Plesioth

value: Green Plesioth. Physiology: Green Plesioth is identical in body structure to the common species. It differs greatly in coloration however, featuring deep green scales along with white and purple striped spines and fins. Abilities: Green Plesioth is a highly adept swimmer, which gives it a distinct advantage over human hunters during underwater combat. It is able to wield its water sac with more skill and precision than the common species. Behavior: Green Plesioth is a highly aggressive wyvern. It has an uncanny ability to determine if there are multiple hunters in an area.
title: Green Plesioth

value: White Monoblos. Physiology: White Monoblos features a bright white carapace covering most of its body. As a result, the flushes of blood to its frill when enraged are significantly more striking. Abilities: The shell of White Monoblos is harder than that of a normal Monoblos, thus increasing its defense. Behavior: White Monoblos behaves essentially the same as its normal counterpart. When not engaged with a hunter it tends to frequently move underground, which can make it hard to track.
title: White Monoblos

value: Anteka. Physiology: Anteka is commonly perceived as the snowy region's counterpart to the Kelbi of the forest and jungle regions. Anteka have a similar build to caribou. Behavior: Anteka are territorial when provoked and will often retaliate by charging at attackers with their antlers.
title: Anteka

value: Ceanataur. Physiology: Similar in appearance to the Hermitaur with sharper claws, leg stubs and a higher-edge shell. The shell on its back was actually grown by the Ceanataur itself. Abilities: It is able to spit a small ball of poisonous fluid at foes. Sometimes, a Ceanataur may wait beneath the ground for prey or a hunter to pass, jumping out of the ground and striking them when they walk over. Behavior: Ceanataur lives mainly in Volcano, where its extremely sharp claws allow it to tunnel through the dense rock. {{-}}
title: Ceanataur

value: Conga. Physiology: They resemble pink gorillas with a face of a hippopotamus, black underbellies and have a blonde patch of hair on top of their head. Abilities: They are known for their ability to charge at hunters, like Bullfangos (but slower), travel in packs, and fart on hunters, they will try to body slam hunters as well. {{-}}
title: Conga

value: Great Thunderbug. Physiology: Despite their name, each Great Thunderbug is actually a colony of Thunderbugs, and their electricity causes them to appear as spheres of blue light. Abilities: Great Thunderbugs are known to change into two different colors: blue and reddish-white. When in their normal and docile state, they are blue, but when attempting to attack, the blue hue becomes a fierce, glowing reddish-white; if they strike a hunter in this state they will cause notable damage. Behavior: Great Thunderbugs are naturally found in the Tower and Jungle.
title: Great Thunderbug

value: Hermitaur. Physiology: Hermaitaur are hermit crab-like monsters with red carapaces and a thick white shell. The shell is grown by the crab itself; only when they grow much larger, into a Daimyo Hermitaur, will they replace it with a Monoblos skull, or into a Black Diablos skull if they grow into the Plum Daimyo Hermitaur. Behavior: When they are left undisturbed, Hermitaurs are most often seen picking through sand with their claws, presumably searching for food. Sometimes, a Hermitaur may wait under the ground for prey or a hunter to pass, jumping out from hiding and striking them when they walk overhead.
title: Hermitaur

value: Popo. Physiology: Popo is a type of large muskox-like Herbivore that resembles a woolly Toxodon or a yak in its body structure. They are covered in long and fluffy fur to protect them from the cold. Behavior: Popo are very docile creatures and will run when one of their members in a group is under attack or when a Wyvern enters their area. They will also group together when a large monster is near, like the Aptonoth in more temperate climates.
title: Popo

value: Remobra. Abilities: They attack by spitting poison and use a variety of melee attacks, such as flicks of the tail and flying tackles. These attacks are not extremely dangerous but offer great annoyance if the hunter is engaged with a larger, more attention-demanding foe. Behavior: They have been spotted in almost every area, but they mainly live in the Volcano and the Swamp. They travel in groups and live in damp, water-rich areas such as the Tower, Jungles, Swamp, and Gorge.
title: Remobra

value: Shakalaka. Physiology: Shakalaka are small, humanoid creatures with green skin. They wear a mask over their heads at all times, so their true faces are unknown. Abilities: Shakalaka should not be underestimated; they can easily take out an unsuspecting hunter with exceedingly quick attacks and inflictions of poison. They are rather clumsy when they attack, but may also be armed with Bounce Bombs and Sleep Bombs. Behavior: Shakalaka are known to inhabit a wide variety of environments. A Shakalaka settlement has been discovered in the Great Forest.
title: Shakalaka

value: Daimyo Hermitaur. Physiology: Daimyo Hermitaur has a large, crab-like body. It is covered in a red and white carapace and wears the skull of a Monoblos as a protective shell. Abilities: It can use its claws as shields to defend itself from attackers, and, as with the Monoblos, can also use them to dig into the earth in order to attack foes from below. It can shoot pressurized jets of water and can use its massive weight to crush enemies. Behavior: Daimyo Hermitaur is a rather passive creature, sometimes failing to take notice of hunters standing only feet in front of it.
title: Daimyo Hermitaur

value: King Shakalaka. Physiology: King Shakalaka is slightly larger than its subjects, but generally looks identical. It wears a BBQ spit upon its head, and weilds a thighbone as a club. Abilities: King Shakalaka is surprisingly strong for its size, able to cause a great deal of damage. It is able to launch fireballs from the BBQ spit atop its head. Behavior: The King Shakalaka is an aggressive leader. Its leadership skills come from its bond shared between it and its minions, but it relies on teamwork in order to bring down an opponent.
title: King Shakalaka

value: Shen Gaoren. Physiology: Identifiable by its grey exoskeleton, towering legs and the gigantic green-yellow balls of acid that it generates and hurls from the rear of its body. Shen Gaoren uses a Lao-Shan Lung skull for protection. Abilities: Unlike Lao-Shan Lung, Shen Gaoren will occasionally pause to attack hunters directly, trying to slam one of its huge claws down upon one. It may do this twice or even 3 times before proceeding along its path through the valley. Behavior: Shen Gaoren is relatively passive by nature. It moves about without noticing hunters; when enraged however, it may attempt to attack hunters with its huge sturdy claws or move its legs and pincers wildly.
title: Shen Gaoren

value: Shogun Ceanataur. Physiology: Shogun Ceanataur's body is covered in a blue carapace. Its head is long and knife-shaped, and features a pair of lengthy antennae. Abilities: Shogun Ceanataur is capable of firing a stream of pressurized liquid from its shell. In addition, it is able to climb on walls and ceilings, and can extend and retract its scythe-like claws. Behavior: Shogun Ceanataur, unlike the Daimyo Hermitaur, demonstrates extreme aggression and will defend itself vigorously if threatened. It is often seen alongside a group of younger Ceanataur.
title: Shogun Ceanataur

value: Lavasioth. Physiology: Lavasioth resembles a bipedal coelacanth fish covered in hardened lava, which forms a tough outer shell. It has two dorsal fins, two pectoral fins, two pelvic fins, and a long, thick tail to help swim through the thick lava it resides in. Abilities: Its attacks and movements are vaguely similar to those of a Plesioth. It can fire huge balls of magma at hunters. Behavior: Lavasioth is a rarely-seen Piscine Wyvern, living and swimming in lava. Like most large monsters, it is territorial and will attack intruders on sight.
title: Lavasioth

value: Plum Daimyo Hermitaur. Physiology: Unlike the common Daimyo Hermitaur, this subspecies sports a purple and white carapace, and wears a Diablos skullshell instead of a Monoblos skullshell. Unlike its red counterpart, the Plum Daimyo Hermitaur is weak against Ice, rather than Thunder. Behavior: Much like its cousin, the Plum Daimyo Hermitaur is docile until provoked or aggravated. Its territory is expansive and thus impossible for it to defend, leading it to be something of a nomad, wandering from place to place in search of food.
title: Plum Daimyo Hermitaur

value: Terra Shogun Ceanataur. Physiology: While considered a Subspecies by the Guild, Terra Shogun Ceanataur is actually the same species as Shogun Ceanataur. Terra Shogun Ceanataurs are particularly aggressive Shogun Ceanataurs that have moved to harsher environments, such as volcanoes and swamps, to live in solitude. Abilities: Terra Shogun Ceanataur, as is normal for a rarer subspecies, is slightly stronger, faster and has more health than the common species. Behavior: Terra Shogun Ceanataur are found in Swamp and the Volcano.
title: Terra Shogun Ceanataur

value: Vespoid Queen. Physiology: The Vespoid Queen looks similar to the average Vespoid but is several times larger, with a more pronounced abdomen and giant, rainbow coloured wings. She has a ridged exoskeleton covering her soft innards and she has a crown-like structure atop her head.
